Adding backgrounds to your MySpace profile is an excellent way to personalize your page and make it stand out from the rest. By following these steps carefully, you can create a unique and visually appealing profile that reflects your personality or interests. **Things You'll Need:** 1. A computer with internet access 2. A MySpace account 3. An image editing software (optional) 4. A background image or pattern 5. Patience and creativity ### **Step 1: Choose Your Background Image** The first step in adding a background to your MySpace profile is selecting an appropriate image or pattern. This could be anything from a solid color to a complex graphic design. Keep in mind that lighter colors tend to work better as they won't obscure text or other elements on your profile. It's also important to choose a high-quality image to ensure optimal visual appeal. If necessary, use an image editing software like Adobe Photoshop or GIMP to resize or crop the image so it fits perfectly within the dimensions of a typical MySpace layout (usually around 960x760 pixels). ### **Step 2: Log into Your MySpace Account** Log into your MySpace account using your username and password. Make sure you are logged into the correct account if you manage multiple profiles. Once logged in, navigate to your profile page by clicking on your display name at the top right corner of the screen. ### **Step 3: Access Profile Customization Options** On your profile page, look for the 'Customize Profile' button, typically located under your profile picture. Clicking this will take you to the customization interface where you can modify various aspects of your profile including its appearance, themes, modules, etc. ### **Step 4: Upload Your Chosen Background Image** In the customization interface, find the section labeled 'Background.' Here, there should be an option to upload your chosen image file. Browse through your computer files until you locate the prepared background image, then select 'Open' to upload it onto your MySpace profile. Depending on the size of the image, it might take several seconds for the upload process to complete. ### **Step 5: Position and Align Your Background Image** After successfully uploading your background image, you'll need to position and align it correctly within the layout. Most platforms provide tools for adjusting horizontal and vertical alignment, along with options to tile the image horizontally, vertically, or both. Experiment with different settings until you achieve a result you're happy with. Remember that tiling can help extend smaller images across larger spaces while maintaining resolution. ### **Step 6: Save Changes and Preview** Once satisfied with your new background, click the 'Save Changes' button to save all modifications made to your profile. After saving changes, MySpace automatically directs you back to your public profile view, enabling you to preview the final output. Check thoroughly to see whether everything looks good - readability of texts, visibility of buttons, no obstruction of any sort. ### **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Avoid overly dark or bright images that may hinder readability of text and strain visitors' eyes. * Test your selected background on various devices and browsers to ensure compatibility and consistent rendering. * Refrain from using copyrighted materials without proper permission; doing so could lead to legal repercussions. * Always keep a backup copy of your original layout before making major alterations. * Be considerate when choosing animated graphics as some users may find them distracting or annoying. * Regularly update your background to maintain freshness and avoid monotony. he exclaimed, laughing softly. Map 1: Boston area today. Few cities can claim such a drastic change in the geography of their land. Starting as early as 1742 the city of Boston began to fill in the shallows near the shore and build such existing structures as Faneuil Hall, the first open market place and town meeting hall in Boston. By the 1820s and into the 1830s hilltops were being scraped and used as landfill. By the 1850s barges ran around the clock bringing in landfill as a growing cosmopolitan Boston filled in the Back Bay section extending the city into the bay created by the Charles River. Questions for Maps 1 & 2 1. Using a large-scale map of the United States, locate Boston and its harbor. 2. Compare the land masses shown on Maps 1 & 2. What changes have been made? Why did Boston have to use landfill in order to expand? 3. Examine Map 2 and locate the Charlestown and Boston peninsulas as well as the roads from Cambridge and Roxbury to Boston Harbor. 4. On Map 2, note the approach to Boston through the harbor. Now locate the various hills and heights surrounding the harbor. Title: How to Change Alarm Ringtone on Android Android devices come with pre-installed alarm ringtones that may not always suit your preferences. Fortunately, you can change the alarm sound to any audio file stored on your device or download new ones from the Google Play Store. This tutorial will guide you through changing the alarm ringtone on your Android device in simple steps. ### Step 1: Access Your Device's Settings Menu To start, unlock your screen and find the 'Settings' app. It usually has a gear icon and is often located in the app drawer or home screen. Tapping on this icon opens up the settings menu where you can customize various aspects of your phone. ### Step 2: Navigate to Sound & Vibration Section Scroll down until you see 'Sound & vibration' or similar wording depending on your device model. Tap on it to proceed. Here, you can manage all sounds related settings like volume levels, notification tones, etc. ### Step 3: Select Clock or Alarms Option Under the 'Sound & vibration' section, look for an option labeled as 'Clock', 'Alarms', or something similar. This option varies across different devices but should be easy to locate since they are generally grouped together. [sits down on the floor and takes off slippers] Mommy um do elephants do doo, doo? It is often used in English to continue a list that is longer than what can be normally written. People most often write "et cetera" as etc.. Very rarely, it is also written "&c" because the ampersand, or the "&", is the same as "et", having been formed by 'e' and 't' being joined into a single letter. It is also the symbol for "and". Some people write it as "ect", but that is wrong since it incorrectly abbreviates "et cetera". The English word "slave" comes from the medieval word for the Slavic peoples of Central Europe and Eastern Europe, because these were the last ethnic group to be captured and enslaved in Central Europe. String conjectures that on the microscopic scale, Einstein's 4D spacetime is a field of Calabi-Yau manifolds, each containing 6 space dimensions curled up, thus not extended into the 3 space dimensions presented to the classical realm. In string theory, each quantum particle is replaced by a 1D string of vibrating energy whose length is the Planck length. As the string moves, it traces width, and thus becomes 2D, a worldsheet. As a string vibrates and moves within the 6D Calabi-Yau space, the string becomes a quantum particle. With this approach, the hypothetical graviton-predicted to explain general relativity-emerges easily. yeah, like last time we said like [maybe something that rocks, I'm not sure] Be quiet and eat your supper, or you're going to bed. Once upon a time there was a little boy named Sam, who was three years old. Sam loved learning about the world around him. One day, Sam heard something mysterious coming from his living room. Thinking it must be something exciting, he ran in to investigate. Sam saw a man sitting in his favorite armchair. The man had bright, shining eyes hidden behind thick glasses. He looked very mysterious. "Hello, my name is Mr. McQueen," said the man. "I've come all the way from a faraway place, and I'd like to teach you something very special." Sam was excited. He had never heard of Mr. McQueen before and he just knew that learning something special had to be fun. "I'm a science teacher," the man said. "But what I want to teach you is something even more special, something that nobody else knows. I want to teach you the secrets of the human eye." Sam wasn't sure what the man meant by this. But he couldn't help but be curious and he eagerly agreed to learn. The man then explained to Sam that the eye has tiny parts called pupils which can see things that are unknown to most people. Title: Understanding Motion: Bowling Ball Edition Imagine you're on a subway train holding a bowling ball. The train starts moving, but the bowling ball stays still relative to you. What's happening here? Let's explore this using some basic physics concepts! Firstly, let's talk about forces - things that cause an object to move or change direction. There are three main forces acting on our stationary bowling ball inside the moving train: gravity (which pulls it down), the normal force (pushing up from the floor), and friction (keeping it from sliding around). Now, imagine trying to tip over the bowling ball by applying a small push. You'll notice that until the push becomes quite strong, the ball just rolls back and forth slightly without actually starting to slide along the floor. This resistance to sliding is called static friction, and when working out problems like these, we often represent it using the letter F. Next, let's consider torque, which is twisting force that causes rotational motion - similar to turning a doorknob or spinning a top. Torque depends on both the applied force and the distance between where the force is applied and the axis of rotation. In this case, if we were to pick the point where the ball touches the ground as our axis, then indeed, the total net torque would appear to be zero... But wait! Something isn't adding up, because intuitively, we know that the ball should start rolling once the train begins accelerating. To resolve this apparent contradiction, let's try changing our perspective: instead of focusing on the point of contact between the ball and the ground, let's consider the center of the ball itself as our new axis. Now suddenly, everything makes more sense! We have a single force (friction) acting on the ball at a certain distance from our chosen axis, creating nonzero torque. As a result, our ball experiences rotational acceleration, causing it to roll along with the train rather than staying put. In summary, even though choosing different points of rotation may seem confusing initially, understanding the basics of forces, torque, and their effects on both linear and rotational motions helps clarify seemingly mysterious phenomena. Just remember that sometimes reevaluating your assumptions leads to clearer insights! That afternoon Malcolm played on the tire swing with Ernie and Bert and Sesame Street Gang. The grave itself, as I came upon it during an early morning stroll, was a rather shapeless brown mound, with an inlaid neat border of white lumps of coral at the base, and enclosed within a circular fence made of split saplings, with the bark left on. A garland of leaves and flowers was woven about the heads of the slender posts--and the flowers were fresh. 'Thus, whether the shadow is of my imagination or not, I can at all events point out the significant fact of an unforgotten grave. When I tell you besides that Jim with his own hands had worked at the rustic fence, you will perceive directly the difference, the individual side of the story. In this Cyberchase video segment, Matt and Jackie must figure out how to construct rectangular lids that match the size of two boxes precisely. They enlist the carpentry skills of one of the Three Little Pigs, who shows them how to use different tools to accurately measure the rectangular lids. For the first box, Jackie finds that she can easily trace the box to make a lid that fits perfectly. The second box cannot be traced, so Matt and Jackie decide to measure the sides of the box and then use the measurements to cut a rectangle of equal size out of a piece of Herculanium. Replicating a shape can be done in different ways, depending on the characteristics of the shape and the tools available. Tracing the outline of a shape is an easy way to know that you have created an exact match. You can use this technique with a pattern to cut fabric for a project such as a pillow or dress. Using a pattern makes it easy to create multiple objects without using measuring tools each time. You can use similar tools like a stencil or a cookie cutter to create duplicate shapes. For larger shapes, however, these techniques may be insufficient. Sometimes it is necessary to take measurements to create an exact match. To start, it is necessary to note the characteristics of the shape, such as the lengths of the sides and the measure of the angles between adjacent sides. It is possible to use width and length measurements to create a parallelogram that is not a rectangle. Therefore, the fact that each of the four angles of a rectangle is ninety degrees is an important detail needed to produce an exact match. It is worth noting that all rectangles are parallelograms, but not all parallelograms are rectangles. Squares are rectangles (and parallelograms), but they also have the special feature of four congruent sides and four 90-degree angles. There are several tools that can be used to create right angles: two include a carpenter's square and a protractor. Mathematical formulas provide another way to verify that an angle is a right angle. If you create a triangle by joining the two sides that comprise the angle, you can check whether the sum of the squares of the two sides is equal to the square of the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle). If it is, then the angle is a right angle. This is the converse of Pythagorean's Theorem. The need for taking measurements arises often in daily life. For example, it is not practical to trace a large window and then bring that piece of paper into the store to buy blinds. Instead, it is more efficient to measure the exact width and height of the window and give these measurements to a store employee before they cut the blinds. It is a about ethics. The word "utilitarianism" comes from the word "utility", which means "usefulness". In most forms of utilitarianism, things that increase human well-being or happiness are called useful. This is not limited to the happiness caused by a single action but also includes the happiness of all people involved and all future consequences. Do you want to see what else is there? I had a mean... hey that doesn't go! Step 1: Reflect on Your Musical Interests Consider what type of music you enjoy listening to and are drawn towards. This will help you determine which instrument aligns best with your musical tastes and passions. By choosing an instrument that resonates with you musically, you're more likely to stay motivated during the learning process. Step 2: Assess Personal Strengths and Weaknesses Identify physical attributes and personal qualities that may influence your choice of instrument. For example, if you have strong lung capacity or a natural singing voice, consider pursuing wind instruments like saxophone or brass instruments such as trumpet. Alternatively, percussion instruments might be suitable if you possess good coordination skills and rhythmic sense. Step 3: Set Clear Goals Determine why you want to learn to play an instrument. Are you looking to join a band? Perform at local events? Or simply wanting to explore a new hobby? Establishing clear goals can guide your decision-making process when selecting an appropriate instrument. Step 4: Consider Space Requirements and Portability Assess the amount of space available at home or elsewhere for practicing and storing your chosen instrument. Some instruments require significant room (e.g., drum sets), while others can be easily transported (e.g., ukulele). Make sure to choose one that fits both your living situation and lifestyle. Step 5: Factor in Financial Constraints Instruments vary greatly in cost, so setting a budget is crucial before making any purchases. Keep in mind additional expenses beyond just buying the instrument itself, including accessories, maintenance, lessons, and sheet music. Research various options within your price range to ensure affordability without compromising quality. Step 6: Consult With Music Professionals Reach out to musicians, teachers, or music store employees for advice on selecting an ideal instrument based on your interests, strengths, goals, and constraints. Their expertise can provide valuable insights into factors you may not have considered previously. Step 7: Try Multiple Instruments Before committing to a single instrument, take advantage of opportunities to try different ones firsthand. Renting or borrowing instruments from friends, family members, or schools allows you to gain hands-on experience and narrow down your choices. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Beginners should start with simpler instruments to minimize frustration and increase chances of success. * Avoid comparing yourself to others; everyone progresses at their own pace. * Practice consistently but realistically; aim for short, frequent sessions rather than marathon practice sessions. * Seek guidance from experienced players or instructors to refine technique and avoid developing bad habits. * Join a community group or online forum dedicated to your chosen instrument to connect with other learners and share experiences. * Stay patient and persistent; mastering an instrument takes time, dedication, and effort. By following these steps and guidelines, you'll be well on your way to finding the perfect instrument to learn and enjoy! Let's see if we can find the body. Which was here first the w-s or the werewolves? Nuclear bombs were invented by the United States with Canadian and British help during World War II to help make that war stop. The destruction of Hiroshima and Nagasaki and the Soviet invasion of Manchuria caused Japan to surrender. They are still the only nuclear weapons that have ever been used in fighting a war. At that time only the United States had the technology needed to make the bomb, but within a few years the Soviet Union had developed it too. In the new Cold War, the United States and the Soviet Union were enemies and each had many nuclear weapons, but they did not dare to use them against each other, either directly or by attacking the other country's allies. Since either country could be completely destroyed by the other's weapons, nuclear war could no longer be limited to the use of only one or two bombs; if they were used anywhere by one side, the other would attack with its own nuclear weapons, and the fighting would almost certainly become greater. This situation came to be known as the "balance of terror," or Mutually Assured Destruction, and stopped conflicts between the two superpowers from leading to a third world war. All beyond the bridge was a plain open field, excepting a few thickets of no great depth, and, consequently, was ground on which the undisciplined forces of the insurgents, deficient as they were in cavalry, and totally unprovided with artillery, were altogether unlikely to withstand the shock of regular troops. Morton, therefore, viewed the pass carefully, and formed the hope, that by occupying two or three houses on the left bank of the river, with the copse and thickets of alders and hazels that lined its side, and by blockading the passage itself, and shutting the gates of a portal, which, according to the old fashion, was built on the central arch of the bridge of Bothwell, it might be easily defended against a very superior force. A special type of cells in the eye senses light for a different purpose than seeing. These cells are called "ganglion" cells. They are located in the retina. They send their information about light to the brain along a different path (the "retinohypothalamic tract"). This information adjusts (synchronizes) the animal's circadian rhythm to nature's light/dark cycle of 24 hours. The system also works for some blind people who cannot see light at all. yeah, and he doesn't do dolls at home. oh, there's just too many toys to choose from? Digging the Earth's Crust This lesson developed by Reach Out! Recommended Age Groups: Later Elementary, Middle School 1. What is the Earth's crust? 2. What is soil? 3. Are there different types of soil? - The Earth's crust is made up of rock and soil material. - Where there are low and dipping areas in the crust, we see where water has collectedas in ponds, lakes, and oceans. - Rivers and streams push their way along different paths on the Earth's crust, cutting their way as they move along. - We can see that there are solid surfaces and water surfaces on our Earth. - The solid surfaces can be very different. Some are hard, others are soft. Massive amounts of rock can be found in tall mountains. Much of the solid surface is made up of what we call soil or dirt. - We build things on soil, plant gardens and crops in soil, dig in soil, and even tunnel under soil. - There are many kinds of soil. We will look at seven different kinds: clay, gravel, humus, loam, rock, sand, - Making Observations - Making Comparisons - Observation Skills - Documenting Findings - Communicating Findings - Pie plate or sturdy paper plates - Access to many different kinds of soil - Clear plastic or glass jars (large baby food jars or peanut butter jars - Labels for soil sample jars - Magnifying glass - Globes and maps - Digging spoons, trowels, spades - Sturdy bag or box to put jars and tools in while going out to dig and find soil samples - Pen or pencil Ample elbow room and place to keep soil sample jars. Procedures and Activity Discuss the following questions and share ideas: - What is the Earth's crust? - What is soil? - Are there different types of soil? Briefly go over these questions to come up with some basic understandings. Review the concepts, principles, and facts listed above. Look at a globe and find large bodies of water and large and well-known rivers that over the years have carved a path in the Earth's crustsuch as the Grand Canyon in Arizona. - Make labels and put one on each jar for clay, gravel, humus, loam, rock, sand, and silt. - Put jars, digging spoons, or trowels in a bag or box. - Go outside and try to get samples of each kind of soil. Put the samples in their own jars. - Inside, get out magnifying glass. Put a little sample of each kind of soil in the pie plate, one sample at a time. Examine it with the magnifying glass. On the handout, write down the color, texture, and materials you see and find in each soil sample. - Add a few teaspoons of water to each pie plate to make the soil damp. Look to see what happens. Do the soil samples take in or absorb the water? Do the soil samples become like mud? What color do you see? Touch each wet soil sample. What does it feel like? Slimy? Sticky? - Leave the wet soil samples out to dry on a shelf near a window. Watch what happens to each type of soil as it dries out. When soil samples are dry, look at them again. Is the color and texture different from when we first dug them up? Which ones seemed to dry faster than the others? - Think about where we would want to use these different kinds of soils. For example, what kind of soil would you want to build a house on? A road? Which soil would be great for gardening or farming? Why? Which soil would you use on a nature path or walking area? Which soil is best for sandboxes? Which soil would you want at the bottom of a pond or lake? Why? - What is the Earth's crust? - What is soil? - What are some different kinds of soil? Listen for evidence of new understandings about what the Earth's crust is, what different kinds of soil there are, and given the characteristics of soils, how we use them for different purposes. - Invite a builder and a civil engineer to come in and talk about soils and how they impact the design and actual construction of houses, larger buildings, and roads. - Invite a farmer to come and share information about soil and what kinds of crops can be grown with different kinds of soil. Or, visit several farmers who raise different crops to learn about their soil and soil - Visit a museum like the University of Michigan's Exhibit Museum to learn about fossils from dinosaurs, fish, plants and even insects. How did these bones and organic materials turn into a rock material? - Look on line at the U.S. Department of Agriculture's Soil Survey Maps of your area (not all maps are on line yet). See whether you can connect the soil type with what the land is being used for. For example, certain types of soil are best for farming; some cannot support heavy buildings; some will not permit the construction of houses with basements, and so forth. - Get smart about soil with S. K. Worm. - Find out How NASA Studies - Check out the National Soil Survey Center's Soil Taxonomy - a description of the 11 major "orders" of soil. - Use the Soil Science Society of America's on-line dictionary to research anything related to soils. - Browse the Natural Resources Conservation Service's Index to Maps, Facts, Careers Related to Lesson Topic - Civil Engineer - Soil that gets hard when baked or fired, and sticky and plastic-like when it gets wet. Light in color. - The Earth's outer surface and layer of material. - Soil made up of lots of little rocks and pebbles. - Soil material that comes from the decaying and decomposing of plants, animals, and insects. It is called "organic," is usually a dark brown or black color, and is a very important part of soil used for growing plants. Decayed materials provide minerals and other food for plants growing in humus. - Soil that is a mix of sand and clay. - Material that is made up from the decaying and decomposing of dead plants, animals or insects. Humus soil is made up of organic material. - A bunch or hunk of soil, pebbles and stone material. Rock is made from soil, stones, and pebbles being heated up and pressed upon for a very long period of time. They are very hard and do not have humus or organic material, so are not any good for growing plants. Rocks have a variety of colors depending on the soil and pebbles they were made of and the minerals that they absorbed over time from water passing over - Loose and light soil that is formed by the breakdown of rocks. - Soil made up of lots of little pebbles, even smaller pebbles than found - The top layer of our Earth that we grow things in, build buildings on, and dig tunnels under. We often call it dirt. Let us know what you think! E-mail our webmaster Last revised 23 Jan 05 One day, May and her mom went to the store. May was excited to explore the shelves. When I was when I just turned four and a half? The air animals and plants use to live is only the first level of the air around the Earth (the troposphere). The day to day changes in this level of air are named weather; the changes between places far away from each other and from year to year are named the climate. Rain and storms are both in this level. Both come about because this part of the air gets colder as it goes up. Cold air becomes thicker and falls, and warm air becomes thinner and goes up. The turning Earth moves the air as well and air moves north and south because the middle of the Earth generally gets more power from the Sun and is warmer than the north and south points. At the same time, air over water (specially very warm water) gets water in it but, because cold air is not able to take in as much water, it starts to make clouds and rain as it gets colder. The way water moves around in a circle like this is called the water cycle. See the end's around it that's why. Do you think he recognized any of the names of the food? I wanted to wear this tomorrow night. DCPIP is often used to measure the electron transport chain in plants. It has a higher redox potential (electron affinity) than the Fe-S complex in cytochrome b and will therefore "steal" electrons from it. This means that cytochrome c does not receive them. The process cuts off the electron supply of photosystem I. A common way to illustrate this change is by the addition of vitamin C solution. This reduces the 'jelly' by transforming it to a colourless mass. This experiment can be carried out to illustrate the effect of changing cell sizes on SA/V (surface area:volume ratio). This is known as Titration. Yes he can take the tires off. Daddy put the apple juice in the refrigerator. that's trouble with it if you... Title: How to Check a Start Capacitor A start capacitor is an essential component in single-phase motors, providing the necessary power boost during startup. Over time, these capacitors can fail or wear out, leading to reduced motor performance or complete failure. This tutorial will guide you through the process of checking a start capacitor using a multimeter, ensuring that your motor remains in top working condition. **Things You'll Need:** 1. Multimeter (digital or analog) with capacitance measurement functionality 2. Screwdriver 3. Safety glasses 4. Rubber gloves 5. Cleaning cloth or brush 6. Replacement capacitor (if needed) **Step 1: Preparation** Before starting, ensure you have a clean, well-lit workspace. Put on safety glasses and rubber gloves to protect yourself from electrical shock or any debris present within the motor housing. **Step 2: Access the Capacitor** Locate the start capacitor on the motor. It may be mounted directly on the motor or situated nearby, connected via wires. Use a screwdriver to remove the mounting screws, covers, or access plates as required. Be cautious when handling sharp edges or metal components. ![Access the Capacitor](https://www.wikihow.com/images/d/da/Check-a-Start-Capacitor-Step-2-Version-2.jpg) *Figure 1 - Removing the cover plate to expose the capacitor.* **Step 3: Identify Terminals and Markings** Identify the terminals on the capacitor-typically labeled 'Common', 'Hermetic', or 'High Voltage'. The other terminal(s) might be marked as 'Start' or simply bear a different color. Also, look for any ratings printed on the side of the capacitor, such as microfarads ( F) and voltage. These values are crucial for verifying compatibility if a replacement becomes necessary. ![Terminal Labels](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/7/79/Check-a-Start-Capacitor-Step-3-Version-2.jpg/aid1806255-v4- Your dad doesn't spill things right in front of the fridge that often. "Instant coffee" is a very quick way to make a cup of coffee to drink. It is made in a factory and sold in jars or packets. First, strong coffee is made using ground coffee and boiling water. Then, the coffee grounds are filtered out. The coffee liquid is dried out until nothing is left except granules (little crunchy pieces), or fine powder. This is then put into jars or packets. As soon as a spoonful of "instant coffee" is added to boiling water, it dissolves in the water to make coffee to drink. The taste can be very different from fresh coffee. Part of the reason for the different taste is that "Robusta" coffee beans are usually used for making instant coffee. Robusta coffee beans do not cost as much as "Arabica". Eight once upon a time two kids saw a large spaceship land from out of the sky. How is blood pressure measured?Blood pressure is the force of blood that pushes against the walls of your arteries. Arteries are blood vessels that carry blood away from your heart to other parts of the body. If the pressure in your arteries becomes too high, you have high blood pressure. High blood pressure is also called hypertension. At each prenantal care visit, your health care provider checks your blood pressure. To do this, she wraps an inflatable cuff around your upper arm. She pumps air into the cuff to measure the pressure in your arteries when the heart contracts (gets tight) and then relaxes. Your blood pressure reading is given as two numbers: the top (first) number is the pressure when your heart contracts and the bottom (second) number is the pressure when your heart relaxes. A healthy blood pressure is 110/80. High blood pressure happens when the top number is 140 or greater, or when the bottom number is 90 or greater Your blood pressure can go up or down during the day. Your provider can re-check a high reading to find out if you have high blood pressure during pregnancy. Later, geneticists discovered that his laws were also true for other living things, even humans. Mendel's findings from his work on the garden pea plants helped to establish the field of genetics. Asian elephants have smoother trunks that end in just one point. Title: How to Feed Discus Fish Discus fish are one of the most popular and beautiful species of freshwater aquarium fish. They require specific care when it comes to feeding due to their unique dietary needs. This tutorial will provide an in-depth guide on how to feed discus fish properly, ensuring they remain healthy and vibrant. **Step 1: Understanding the Dietary Needs of Discus Fish** Before you start feeding your discus fish, it's crucial to understand what kind of food they need. Discus are omnivores that primarily eat proteins such as brine shrimp, bloodworms, daphnia, and other small insects in the wild. However, they also consume plant matter. In captivity, a high-quality commercial discus pellet or flake food should form the basis of their diet, supplemented with frozen or live foods. Key Tip: Always opt for high-quality, specialized discus fish food. These products contain necessary nutrients specifically tailored for these fish. **Step 2: Choosing the Right Food** There is a wide variety of commercially available discus fish food. Here are some options: - **Pellets:** Look for sinking pellets designed explicitly for discus. They usually have higher protein content than general-purpose fish pellets. - **Flakes:** While less common, there are specialty flake foods made for discus. Make sure they sink quickly since discus prefer eating from the bottom of the tank. - **Frozen Foods:** Bloodworms, brine shrimp, and daphnia can be bought frozen. Defrost before feeding. These offer excellent sources of protein and other essential nutrients. - **Live Foods:** Live brine shrimp, earthworms, or micro worms can be used but ensure they come from reputable sources to avoid introducing diseases into the tank. Guideline: A balanced diet includes both commercial discus food and occasional treats of frozen or live foods. **Step 3: Determining How Much to Feed** The amount of food depends on several factors including the number of fish, size of the tank, and type of food. As a rule of thumb, feed only what your fish can consume within five minutes, once or twice daily. Overfeeding can lead to poor water quality and health issues. Key Tip: If after five minutes, there's still food left, reduce the quantity during the next feeding session. **Step 4: Timing Your Feedings** Discus do well with regular feeding schedules. Once or twice a day works best. Try to maintain consistent timing. Many hobbyists find success with morning and evening feedings. Guideline: Regularly scheduled meals mimic natural conditions and help regulate digestion. **Step 5: Observing Your Fish During Feeding Time** Pay attention to how your fish behave while eating. All should appear interested and active. If any seem lethargic or disinterested, this could indicate illness. Key Tip: Monitoring feeding behavior allows early detection of potential health problems. **Step 6: Cleaning Up After Meals** Remove uneaten food promptly after meal times to prevent decay which affects water quality. Use a gravel vacuum if necessary. Guideline: Maintaining cleanliness reduces the risk of disease and promotes overall tank health. Remember, providing proper nutrition plays a significant role in keeping your discus fish healthy and happy. Patience and consistency are vital throughout this process. Happy fishkeeping! In drip irrigation, water drips directly to the roots of plants from pipes which have small holes or special emitters spaced along the pipe. Drip irrigation is more efficient than other irrigation methods because water is applied directly or close to plants' roots i.e. where it is needed. Thus, it uses less water, reduces leeching of soil nutrients and erosion of top soil. XXX going to have the chicken little. Baby no you don't eat the baby's face. And then he went to tie his shoes. Stack based languages look at some of the program's memory like a stack of cards. There are very few things that can be done with a stack. A data item can be put on the top of the stack. This operation is generally called "push". A data item can be removed from the top of the stack. This is called a "pop". You can look at the item at the top of the stack without removing it. A CERTAIN Shepherd had a Dog, upon whose fidelity he relied very much; for whenever he had occasion to be absent himself, he committed the care and tuition of the flock to the charge of his Dog; and, to encourage him to do his duty cheerfully, he fed him constantly with sweet curds and whey, and sometimes threw him a crust or two. Yet, notwithstanding this, no sooner was his back turned, but the treacherous cur fell foul of the flock, and devoured the sheep, instead of guarding and defending them. The Shepherd being informed of this, was resolved to hang him; and the Dog, when the rope was about his neck, and he was just going to be hung, began to expostulate with his master, asking him, why he was so unmercifully bent against him, who was his own servant and creature, and had only committed two or three crimes, and why he did not rather execute vengeance upon the Wolf, who was a constant and declared enemy? Title: How to Help an Emotionally Disturbed Dog Introduction: Emotionally disturbed dogs can exhibit a range of behaviors that are distressing for both the dog and their owner. These behaviors may include extreme fear, anxiety, aggression, or self-harm. Helping an emotionally disturbed dog requires patience, consistency, and a commitment to understanding and addressing the root causes of their behavior. This guide will provide you with steps to help your emotionally disturbed dog find comfort and peace. Step 1: Consult a Veterinarian Before beginning any behavior modification program, consult a veterinarian to rule out medical issues contributing to your dog's emotional disturbance. Medical conditions such as hypothyroidism, epilepsy, or chronic pain can cause changes in behavior. Your vet might recommend medication, which could be beneficial when combined with behavioral training. Step 2: Observe and Document Behaviors Carefully observe your dog's body language, facial expressions, and actions during different situations. Keep a journal noting triggers, durations, and frequencies of problematic behaviors. Understanding these patterns is crucial for developing an effective intervention plan tailored to your dog's needs. Key Tip: Remember that every dog is unique; what works for one may not work for another. Patience and flexibility are essential throughout this process. Step 3: Create a Safe Space Provide your dog with a designated safe space where they can retreat from stressors. This area should be quiet, comfortable, and free from disturbances. Use positive reinforcement techniques to associate this space with safety and calmness. Guideline: Never force your dog into their safe space; instead, encourage them to enter voluntarily by placing treats or toys inside. Step 4: Implement Desensitization Training Desensitize your dog to identified triggers gradually. Start by exposing them to diluted versions of the trigger at a distance where they do not react negatively. Gradually decrease the distance over time while maintaining a non-reactive state. Reward calm responses with praise or treats. Key Tip: Move slowly through this process; pushing too quickly can exacerbate fears rather than alleviate them. Step 5: Practice Counterconditioning Techniques Counterconditioning involves replacing negative associations with positive ones. For example, if your dog becomes anxious during car rides, associate car trips with enjoyable experiences like going to a park or receiving special treats. Over time, this association can reduce anxiety associated with cars. Step 6: Engage in Regular Exercise Exercise plays a significant role in managing emotional disturbances. Daily walks, play sessions, or structured activities like agility training can significantly improve mood and reduce undesirable behaviors stemming from boredom or excess energy. Guideline: Always ensure exercises are suitable for your dog's age, breed, and physical condition. Step 7: Provide Mental Stimulation Mental stimulation is equally important as physical exercise. Food puzzles, scent games, or short training sessions can keep your dog mentally engaged and contribute to overall wellbeing. Step 8: Seek Professional Guidance If progress stalls or behavior worsens, consider hiring a certified animal behaviorist or professional trainer experienced in treating emotionally disturbed dogs. They can offer personalized strategies based on your dog's specific needs and challenges. Conclusion: Helping an emotionally disturbed dog takes time, effort, and understanding. By following these steps, observing your dog closely, and responding appropriately to their individual needs, you can make substantial strides toward improving their emotional health and quality of life. Students need to be able to recognize letters and sounds that those letters make in order to read and understand words. lesson focuses on short a (a = /a/). Students will learn to recognize a that a = /a/. book: "A Cat Nap" (Phonics Readers) sentence strip with "Anna asks for apples" large bold letters - Explain that our mouth makes different formations or movements in order to make different sounds - have students say /a/ a few times so that they become familiar with the movement their mouth makes when saying short a (a = /a/) - Display the sentence strip and read "Anna asks for apples" for them, then have them read it with you - Have students say the sentence stretching out the /a/. "Aaaannaaa aaaasks for aaaapples." - Have students identify what words they here /a/ in. Do you hear /a/ in mat or mit? Bat or bug? Strap or stuck? Pass or hut? Etc. - Pass out pencils and primary paper. Now teach the students to make an "a". "Start at the fence, circle down to the sidewalk, come back up and touch the fence, and move straight back down to the side walk." Have them practice this a few times. - Read the book "A Cat Nap". After reading the book once read it again but this time stop at the end of each sentence and have the students identify the words they heard that have the a = /a/ sound in them. - For further assessment have the students identify different objects in the classroom that have the a = /a/ sound in their name. [a silver man fell; MOT sets it back up] Moments make things turn or rotate. They are caused by forces but are not forces themselves. Like forces, moments have a direction. We say they are either clockwise or anti-clockwise, to show which way they will make something turn. The bigger the force causing the turning effect the bigger the moment will be. The further the force is from the pivot the bigger the moment will be. The size of a moment can be calculated using: Moment = Force x Distance - Force is measured in newtons, N. - Distance is measured in either m or cm. - If the distance is in m then the moment will be measured in Nm. - If the distance is in cm then the moment will be measured in Ncm. (Note: The force needs to be at right angles to the lever or rotating object.) Here are some pictures involving moments. For each picture: Click on the moment and the direction that you think is correct then mark your answer. In many situations there is more than one moment acting. To find the net or resultant moment the moments have to be added or subtracted, depending on their direction. Here is a worked example: Moment due to A = 20 x 2 = 40 Nm anti-clockwise Moment due to B = 10 x 1 = 10 Nm anti-clockwise Moment due to C = 20 x 3 = 60 Nm clockwise The total anticlockwise moment is 40 +10 = 50 Nm. The total clockwise moment is 60 Nm. So the resultant moment is 60 Nm - 50 Nm = 10 Nm clockwise. Now here is one for you to try. Fill the correct numbers into the boxes: = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/24/5965PC.cha = = = Dick had no end of a job, as you know, to get him undone." Creating a birthing center in Animal Jam is an exciting and creative way to role-play as a medical professional or caretaker for newborn animals. This guide will walk you through the process of setting up your own birthing center, with clear explanations for each step and helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Choose Your Den Layout ------------------------------- The first step in creating your birthing center is choosing a den layout that suits your needs. You can purchase dens from the Diamond Shop, or obtain them through various methods such as membership perks or rare item hunts. For a birthing center, consider a larger den with multiple rooms to accommodate different stages of labor and recovery. A three-room den would be ideal, with one room for check-ins, another for delivery, and a third for postpartum care and rest. Key tip: Keep functionality in mind when selecting furniture and decorations. Opt for pieces that provide ample seating and storage space. ### Step 2: Design Each Room Now that you have chosen your den layout, it's time to design each room according to its purpose. #### Check-in Room This room should include comfortable seating areas where expectant mothers can wait their turn for examination and preparation before giving birth. Decorate this area with soothing colors and calming patterns to create a relaxing atmosphere. Add plants, paintings, and other nature-themed items to promote tranquility. #### Delivery Room Design this room with clean lines and bright lighting for optimal visibility during childbirth. Use white or light-colored furnishings to convey sterility and hygiene. Consider adding hospital beds, incubators, and medical equipment like stethoscopes and ultrasound machines (though these are purely cosmetic). Remember that actual medical procedures cannot be performed within Animal Jam. #### Postpartum Care & Rest Room After delivering their babies, mothers need privacy and comfort while they recover. Furnish this room with cozy seating options, soft blankets, and pastel color schemes. Including cribs or bassinets provides a safe place for newborns to sleep while being close to their mothers. Step 3: Create Signage ---------------------- Clear signage ensures visitors know exactly where to go upon entering your birthing center. Use large text and bold colors to make signs easy to read. Place arrows pointing towards each room, and label them clearly: "Check-In," "Delivery," and "Postpartum Care." Additionally, consider including informational signs about pregnancy, childbirth, and baby care to educate fellow players. Step 4: Host Roleplays --------------------- Once your birthing center is set up, invite friends and other players to participate in roleplays. Encourage them to act out scenarios involving prenatal appointments, labor, delivery, and postpartum care. Be sure to establish ground rules for appropriate behavior and language before starting any roleplay sessions. Key guideline: Always maintain a respectful environment by reminding participants that Animal Jam is intended for all ages. Refrain from using graphic or mature themes during roleplays. By following these steps and adhering to the provided tips and guidelines, you'll have successfully created a welcoming and educational birthing center within Animal Jam! Title: How to Be a Fairy Introduction: Fairies are mythical creatures that have been depicted in various cultures as small, magical beings with wings. While there is no scientific evidence supporting their existence, many people enjoy pretending to be fairies or incorporating them into imaginative stories and games. This tutorial will provide you with steps on how to embody the spirit of a fairy through appearance, behavior, and creative activities. Remember, this guide aims to encourage imagination, self-expression, and playfulness - so let your inner child shine! Step 1: Dressing Like a Fairy 1. Choose flowy clothing in natural colors such as greens, blues, browns, and golds. Select materials like silk, chiffon, lace, or organza to mimic the delicate look of insect wings. 2. Add accessories like crowns made from flowers, vines, or leaves. You can also wear necklaces, bracelets, or anklets adorned with nature-inspired charms. 3. Paint your face using light, shimmery makeup to create a youthful glow. Opt for pastel eyeshadows and lipsticks to enhance your features without being too dramatic. 4. Create fairy wings using wire, tulle, and fabric glue. Attach the wings securely to a comfortable harness or clip them onto a hairband for easy removal. 5. Key tip: Keep your outfit age-appropriate while maintaining a whimsical feel. Avoid overtly sexualized costumes if you plan to participate in family-friendly events or public spaces. Step 2: Adopting Fairylike Habits 1. Develop a love for nature by spending time outdoors, gardening, or studying plants and animals. This connection will help you understand the world from a fairy's perspective. 2. Practice kindness, compassion, and generosity towards others. Fairies are often portrayed as helpful and giving creatures who bring joy to those around them. 3. Cultivate curiosity and creativity by exploring new hobbies, reading books, or engaging in artistic pursuits. Fairies are known for their inventiveness and appreciation for beauty. 4. Hone your observational skills by paying attention to details in your environment. Notice changes in weather patterns, plant growth, and animal behaviors. 5. Key guideline: Always respect local laws, regulations, and private property when interacting with nature. Refrain from damaging plants, disturbing wildlife, or trespassing on other people's land. Step 3: Engaging in Fairy Activities 1. Learn about different types of fairies from various cultures and incorporate elements from these traditions into your persona. Examples include Irish clurichauns, Welsh Tylwyth Teg, or Russian domoviks. 2. Create art inspired by fairies, such as paintings, drawings, sculptures, or written stories. Share your creations with friends, family members, or online communities. 3. Host tea parties with fellow fairy enthusiasts where you can dress up, exchange gifts, and engage in imaginative conversations. 4. Organize scavenger hunts in parks or forests to search for items related to fairies, like shiny stones, feathers, or interesting sticks. 5. Participate in LARP (Live Action Role Play) events centered around fantasy themes, including those involving fairies. These gatherings allow you to act out scenes, solve puzzles, and make new connections. 6. Key tip: Respect the boundaries and comfort levels of others during group activities. Not everyone may share your enthusiasm for fairies, so approach discussions with sensitivity and openness. Mary was nervous, her heart trembled like thunder in her chest. She looked at the icy mask in her hand, it looked scary but she had to wear it. She took a deep breath and put it on her face. It was cold against her skin and made her shiver. Outside the sky was dark and clouds filled the sky. She took a deep breath. She could feel the icy wind against her face and knew that it was going to be a cold day. She grabbed the mask in her hands and started walking, trembling a bit with every step. DESCRIPTION, SENSES: Barred owls are large owls with round heads and NO ear tufts. They are brownish-gray with brown and white bars across their chest. (Thats why theyre called "barred" owls.) Their legs are covered with feathers. The barred owls eyes are dark brown, while most other owls have yellow eyes. Their beaks are sharp, but very small, so they are almost covered up by facial feathers. Owls can see very well at night, but they can also see during the day. They have a special inner eyelid which blocks bright sunlight - owl sunglasses! Most birds have eyes on their sides of their head. This helps them to see sideways and backwards. Owls, however, have both eyes in the front. This gives them good depth perception (helps them to tell how far away something is). Unfortunately, it means they cant see sideways or backwards. To deal with this, owls can turn their head ALMOST all the way around. But they cant spin their head in a full circle; their head would twist off! Owl ears are just holes on the sides of their head covered with feathers. These holes are higher on one side than the other. This helps the owl tell if a sound is coming from above or below. When you hear a sound, and you cant tell where its coming from, dont you tilt your head to listen better? The owl doesnt have to tilt his head. His ears are tilted already! You also cup your hand over your ear to help you hear, right? Owls dont have hands, but they can fluff the feathers around their ear holes to make a little funnel, just like your hand. The ear tufts that some owls have are NOT ears. Theyre just feathers that might scare away predators. The barred owls sense of smell is probably not that strong. They even eat skunks. FLIGHT & HUNTING: Barred owls are often out at night, dawn or dusk, but can be seen during the day. When hunting, they usually perch in a tree and watch and listen for prey. Then they swoop down and grab it with their talons (claws). Their toes are special. Normally, two go forward and two go back. This helps them get a good grip on their prey. But they can turn a back toe forward if they want. This may help them to hold on to a perch tree. Barred owls (like all owls) are carnivores (meat-eaters). They can eat mice, squirrels, foxes, rabbits, bats, small birds, other owls, snakes, lizards, fish, fiddler crabs, and bugs. Since they eat the whole animal (except for the wings of birds), they swallow a lot of fur and bones they cant digest. Owls will regurgitate (re-gir-ji-tate) or vomit pellets of fur and bones. If you find a pile of little furry balls under a tree, you have found an owls perch tree or eating spot. If you pick the pellet apart, you can see the tiny bones and fur of the animals it has eaten. Barred owls like to sit on trees which have small branches and moss to hide and camouflage them. When they fly, they flap their wings a lot, instead of soaring like a vulture. This is because they have special soft feathers. Soft feathers are quiet; this helps the owl to sneak up on night animals. But soft feathers are not very good at catching the wind. The owl is a good hunter, but he cant rest and soar. He must work hard to fly. NESTING AND RAISING BABIES: Barred owls live in old forests near marshes or ponds. They nest in the big holes left by broken branches. They dont really build a nest in the hole, but may put some soft feathers or grass in the bottom. If they cant find a tree hole, they may use an old nest left by a hawk, crow, or squirrel. They hardly ever build their own nests. The same male and female stay together on a 1 square mile territory all year. They do not migrate. Each year they use the same nest. The mother lays 2-3 white, almost round eggs. When the babies are born, they are covered with white fluffy down and their eyes stay closed for a whole week. It is a long time before they can fly, about 40 days. Before this, they crawl out of the nest and sit together on a branch to look around. If they fall out of the tree, they can usually climb back up using their strong beaks and talons. The parents hunt for their food and tear meat into small pieces they can eat. After the babies learn to fly, they follow their parents around and learn to hunt. The parents will still feed them for a long time. The mother is a little bigger than the father, but otherwise, males, females, and teenagers look just alike. OWLS AND PEOPLE: Barred owls live all over the eastern U.S., and are very common in Florida. Their calls sound like "who cooks for you? Who cooks for you all?" Since barred owls like to live near water, they may get tangled in fishing line someone has left behind. Sometimes they are hit by cars while looking for prey near a road. NEVER try to touch or get close to an owl. They are predators with strong talons and will hurt you if you get too close. It is against the law to have an owl as a pet or to have owl feathers without a permit from the government. Its better to watch and learn and enjoy the owl free. He quickly realized that the vendor was selling great food, despite the place being so dirty. [Nina finds an airplane in the box.] [does same thing, laughs on and on.] Title: How to Avoid Bedhead: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Bedhead can be an unwanted and unprofessional appearance that affects many people, especially those with longer hair. It occurs when your hair becomes tangled or messy while you sleep, leading to a disheveled look upon waking up. This guide will provide you with actionable steps and helpful tips to avoid bedhead and maintain well-groomed hair throughout the night. Step 1: Brush Your Hair Before Going to Sleep Rationale: Properly brushing your hair before sleeping ensures that there are no knots or tangles that could turn into bedhead during the night. Use a high-quality brush with soft bristles to gently detangle any stubborn areas without causing damage to your hair. Key Tip: Be gentle while brushing; excessive pulling may lead to breakage and split ends. Start from the bottom and work your way up to minimize pain and potential harm. Step 2: Secure Long Hair With a Loose Ponytail or Braid Rationale: Keeping your hair tied back reduces friction between strands as you move around at night, preventing tangling and subsequent bedhead formation. Opt for loose hairstyles like low ponytails or braids to prevent tension on your scalp and avoid damaging your hair follicles. Guideline: Refrain from using tight elastics or harsh clips that might leave dents or creases in your hair. Instead, choose fabric-covered scrunchies or soft hair ties. Step 3: Protect Your Hairstyle With a Silk or Satin Bonnet/Pillowcase Rationale: Both silk and satin materials create less friction compared to cotton pillowcases, reducing frizziness and maintaining smoothness overnight. Using a bonnet or switching to a satin/silk pillowcase is particularly beneficial if you have curly hair prone to becoming dry and unmanageable. Key Tips: - Make sure the bonnet fits snugly but comfortably over your head, covering all your hair. - If opting for a satin/silk pillowcase, ensure it's large enough to accommodate your entire head without slipping off during the night. Step 4: Apply Leave-In Conditioner or Anti-Frizz Serum (Optional) Rationale: Applying these products prior to tying up your hair adds extra protection against friction and moisture loss, further minimizing the risk of bedhead. Moreover, they help keep your locks hydrated and manageable throughout the day. Guidelines: - Choose lightweight formulas that won't weigh down your hair or make it appear greasy. - Focus on applying product mainly on the lengths and ends rather than the roots to avoid looking oily. Step 5: Adjust Your Sleep Position Rationale: Certain sleeping positions contribute more significantly to bedhead development. For instance, lying on one side can cause flattening and indentation marks along the crown area. To mitigate this issue, try changing positions frequently or consider investing in a specially designed pillow for hair care. Key Tips: - If possible, sleep on your back to reduce contact between your hair and the pillow surface. - Experiment with different pillows explicitly made for hair preservation, such as contoured ones that promote natural alignment and alleviate pressure points. Conclusion: By following these simple yet effective steps, you can significantly decrease the likelihood of encountering bedhead upon waking up. Remember, consistency is crucial - incorporating these habits into your daily routine will yield optimal results over time. Happy sleeping! Clear iPhone Notifications All at Once: A Comprehensive Guide The iPhone's notification system is designed to keep you informed about important updates from your apps. However, sometimes you may find yourself with an overwhelming number of notifications that can be distracting or unnecessary. This guide will show you how to clear all iPhone notifications at once, providing detailed steps and helpful insights along the way. Why Clear Notifications? ------------------------ There are several reasons why clearing notifications might be beneficial: 1. **Focus:** Reduce distractions by removing clutter from your lock screen or notification center. 2. **Privacy:** Hide sensitive information displayed in previous push notifications. 3. **Organization:** Maintain a clean interface for easier navigation through app updates. Before we begin, let us clarify that there isn't a one-click solution to delete all notifications simultaneously across every single application on your device due to potential data loss concerns (you wouldn't want to miss an urgent message because it got cleared accidentally). Nevertheless, this guide offers two methods-manual clearing per app and group deletion-to efficiently manage multiple notifications at once. ### Method 1: Manually Clearing Notifications Per App This method allows you to selectively remove notifications category by category within specific applications. Here's what you need to do: #### Step 1: Access Your Notification Center Swipe down from the top edge of any screen (except when using certain full-screen apps like Camera) to open the Notification Center. If your phone has Face ID, swiping down from the upper-right corner will also work. #### Step 2: Identify the Applications with Multiple Notifications Look for apps displaying more than one alert under their respective headings. These headers usually include the app icon, name, and time of the most recent update. #### Step 3: Enter X-Mode (Clearing Mode) Perform a long press (also known as a "force touch") on any app heading showing multiple alerts until a 'X' button appears on its right side. This action enters X-mode, enabling you to mass-clear notifications for that particular app. ![Long Press on App Heading](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/a/ad/Clear-All-Notifications-at-Once-on-the-iPhone-690x388.jpg/aid5471368-v4- When another time when he punched me I'll punch him back. Most adults know the rules of walking safely on the road. But children have to be taught how to stay safe. This sheet tells you how to teach your child to be a safe pedestrian. Young children don't yet have the skills needed to cross a street safely. This is especially true of kids under 10 years old. Keep in mind that young children: Are impulsive. They often run out into the street unexpectedly, perhaps chasing a ball. Have limited attention spans and can be easily distracted. And they may have trouble putting together all of the pieces involved with crossing the street safely. Are shorter than adults. Most are under 4 feet tall. So they may find it hard to see above or around obstacles such as parked cars or bushes. Also, drivers may find it hard to see them. Have limited side (peripheral) vision. This means a child looking straight ahead may have trouble seeing movement to the side. So, a child must be taught to turn his or her head when looking for traffic. Have trouble locating sounds. A child may not be able to tell what direction a car is coming from. Have limited perception of speed and distance. A child may not be able to tell how fast a car is going, or how far away a car is. May not realize that just because they see a driver does not mean the driver can see them. May not understand the danger of traffic. Children may not grasp the idea that a vehicle could hurt or kill them. There are important "Dos" and "Don'ts" that all pedestrians should follow. Teach these to your child to keep him or her safe when walking on and near the road. They include: DO recognize and obey all traffic signs and signals. This includes "Walk" and "Don't Walk" signs. Be sure the child knows to continue to walk even if a light changes to "Don't Walk" while they are in the crosswalk. DO walk on the sidewalk whenever possible. If there is no sidewalk, pedestrians should walk as far away from traffic as possible, facing traffic. DO stop and look before crossing driveways and alleys. DO pay extra attention in bad weather. Drivers may find it harder to see walkers, and it may be harder for drivers to stop. DO always listen to crossing guards and follow their signals. DO look for white lights on the back of a car that indicate the car is backing up. DO pay special attention at railroad crossings. Remind your child that trains can be very dangerous. DO only cross streets at corners or at crosswalks. DO be alert and aware at all times. DO cross streets carefully. Every walker should: Stop at the curb at a corner or crosswalk. Look left, right, and left again, making sure no vehicles are approaching. Make eye contact with any nearby drivers before crossing. Make sure cars have come to a complete stop before crossing. Walk, not run, across the crosswalk, still looking left and right. DON'T cross the street between parked cars. DON'T ever run into the street. DON'T play in streets, parking lots, driveways, or unfenced yards by the street. When walking near a road with your child: Use the time to teach your child about pedestrian safety. Hold your child's hand at all times. Have your child wear bright colors so drivers can see him or her more easily. In low light, children should wear reflective materials. Teach your child to make safety decisions. As your child gets older, help him or her develop safety judgment. For instance, while crossing the street, explain why you think it's safe to cross at a particular time. Ask if your child agrees. If your child walks to school: Walk with your child the first several times to help him or her find a direct route with few street crossings. Have your child follow that same route each day. Encourage your child to walk with an older student or a buddy. As a driver, you can take steps to make it safer for children walking near the road. These steps include the following: Reduce your speed, especially in residential neighborhoods and near schools. Check for children when backing out of a driveway or parking space. Also look for children who may suddenly dart out into the road. Don't be distracted while driving. Avoid using a cell phone, eating, or playing with the radio while driving. Stop completely at stop signs. And don't cover the crosswalk. If you see a situation that might be dangerous for pedestrians, report it to your local government or police department. Let's think about belonging to a club or group as our example. Imagine that we have three different clubs: Club A, Club B, and Club C. To say that a person belongs to Club A or Club B means that they belong to either one of those clubs, or possibly even both. Now let's add Club C into the mix. It makes sense that anyone who already belonged to Club A or Club B also now belongs to the combined group of all three clubs, which includes members from Club A, Club B, and Club C. So, the membership of Club A or Club B is part of, or a subset of, the combined membership of Club A, Club B, and Club C. Now, let's connect this back to the problem from before: Suppose x is a member of set A or set B. That means x could be a member of set A, or set B, or both. When we include elements from set C along with A and B, x will definitely fit within this bigger set since x has been confirmed to be part of set A or set B. Therefore, A B is indeed a subset of A B C because every element in A B satisfies the condition of being in A B C. Title: How to Change Your Phone Number on Uber for iPhone or iPad Uber is a popular ride-hailing service that allows users to request rides using their smartphones. If you need to change your phone number associated with your Uber account for any reason, this tutorial will guide you through the process on both iPhones and iPads. **Step 1:** Open the Uber app on your device The first step is to launch the Uber application on either your iPhone or iPad. The icon has a black background with a white circle containing a small bit of cursive text that says 'Uber.' Once opened, proceed to Step 2. **Step 2:** Log into your account (if necessary) If you're already logged in, you can skip this step. However, if you find yourself at the login screen, enter your registered email address and password to access your account. This action ensures we are working within the correct user profile. **Step 3:** Access the menu Tap the hamburger icon (three horizontal lines stacked together), usually located in the top left corner of the screen. It will reveal a side panel with various options related to managing your Uber account. **Step 4:** Navigate to Settings Scroll down until you see 'Settings,' then tap on it. Here, you'll be able to customize different aspects of your Uber experience, such as payment methods, notification preferences, and more importantly, personal information. **Step 5:** Tap 'Personal Information' Under settings, look for an option labeled 'Personal Info' or similar wording - depending upon updates made by Uber since writing this article. By tapping here, you gain access to edit your name, photo, contact details, etc., including the phone number linked to your Uber account. **Step 6:** Select 'Phone Numbers' Within Personal Information, there should be another subheading titled 'Phone numbers'. Tap on this to view all telephone numbers attached to your Uber account. **Step 7:** Remove existing phone number To replace your current phone number, you must first delete it from your account. You'll notice a trash bin or delete button next to the listed phone number(s). Press it and confirm deletion when prompted. Please note that removing your only phone number may log you out immediately; keep other devices handy where you can still access your account via email verification codes. **Step 8:** Add new phone number After successfully removing the old number, select "+Add phone number" at the bottom of the page. Enter your new phone number carefully, making sure no errors occur during input. Double-check digits before moving forward. **Step 9:** Verify new phone number Once entered, Uber typically sends a verification code to the newly added number via SMS. Retrieve this code and type it back into the verification field provided within the app. Confirmation indicates successful linkage of the new number. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Always ensure accuracy while entering the new phone number. A wrongly typed digit could lead to issues verifying the new number. * Remember, once deleted, you might lose immediate access to your Uber account unless you have alternative means of logging in, like via email. * Keep your new phone close because timely receipt of the verification code expedites the entire process. * Changing your phone number doesn't affect active trips or past ride history. Everything else remains intact under your original account credentials. An old blackbird minister, black-frocked and solemn, delivered a sermon to him from a branch. "We must obey God's commandments. Once or twice he drew near Idalie, and tried to disengage her from the crowd; but each time he saw the Princess watching him stealthily, while his young bride, with feminine prudence, avoided every opportunity of conversing apart with him. Ladislas could ill endure this. He began to fancy that he had a thousand things to say, and that their mutual safety depended on his being able to communicate them to her. He wrote a few lines hastily on the back of a letter with a pencil, conjuring her to find some means of affording him a few minutes' conversation, and telling her that if this could not be done before, he should take occasion, while the rest of the company were otherwise occupied, to steal from them that evening to the larger temple, and there await her joining him, for that everything depended on his being able to speak to her. He scarcely knew what he meant as he wrote this; but, driven by contradiction and impatience, and desirous of learning exactly how she meant to conduct herself on the Princess's disembarking at Naples, it seemed to him of the last importance that his request should be complied with. [takes all the pieces off the yellow wand] Creating a block hologram in Minecraft is an impressive way to add some advanced technology to your world. This can be done using various methods, but one popular approach involves commands and redstone contraptions. Here's our step-by-step guide: ### Things You'll Need: * A Minecraft world with cheats enabled (to allow command usage) * Basic knowledge of Redstone mechanics * Familiarity with commands, particularly `/setblock` and `/data merge` ### Step 1: Create a Scoreboard Objective Firstly, we need to create a scoreboard objective that will track player interaction with our holographic blocks. Type this command into the chat window: ```/scoreboard objectives add interact minecraft.used:minecraft.block_break Title: How to Enable the Attribute Editor Tab in Active Directory on Windows Introduction: The Attribute Editor tab is a powerful tool in Microsoft's Active Directory Users and Computers (ADUC) console that allows administrators to view and modify various attributes associated with objects such as users, groups, and computers. However, this feature isn't enabled by default. This tutorial will guide you through enabling the Attribute Editor tab in Active Directory on Windows systems. Step 1: Open the Run dialog box Press 'Windows + R' keys simultaneously or right-click on the Start button and select 'Run'. The run dialog box will appear. Step 2: Type in 'dsa.msc' and press Enter In the text field provided in the run dialog box, type 'dsa.msc' without quotes and hit enter. This command opens the Active Directory Users and Computers snap-in. Step 3: Right-click on an empty space within the console tree Locate an open area within the left pane where all your domains are listed under 'Active Directory Users and Computers', then right-click on it. A context menu should pop up. Step 4: Select 'Advanced Features' from the Context Menu From the dropdown list, click on 'Advanced Features'. Enabling this option changes the view mode of ADUC, displaying additional tabs including our target - the Attribute Editor tab. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Always ensure you have sufficient permissions before attempting these steps. Typically, only domain admins can enable advanced features. * Changing certain attributes carelessly could lead to unexpected behavior or issues; always double-check any modifications made via the Attribute Editor tab. * Remember, just because you can edit something doesn't mean you should. Only change what is necessary following best practices. Step 5: Navigate to an object and open its properties window Now navigate to any user, group, or computer account, right-click on it, and choose 'Properties'. For example, go to 'Users', find JohnDoe, right-click, and select 'Properties'. Step 6: Check if the Attribute Editor tab is visible After completing Step 4 correctly, you should see an extra tab labeled 'Attribute Editor' at the top of the Properties window. If it's there, congratulations! You successfully enabled the attribute editor tab. Troubleshooting: If after performing Steps 1-4, you still don't see the 'Attribute Editor' tab, try restarting the server or PC. Sometimes, closing and reopening the MMC might also solve the problem. Conclusion: By following this simple yet thorough process, you now know how to reveal one of Active Directory's most useful but hidden features - the Attribute Editor tab. It provides granular control over every aspect of directory objects, making management tasks more efficient and precise. But remember great power comes with great responsibility, so exercise caution when using it. Formatting a Fat32 Drive with Mac Formatting a drive erases all data on the drive and prepares it for use with a new operating system or file structure. This guide will walk you through formatting a drive as FAT32 using your Mac computer. FAT32 is a universal file format that can be read by both Windows and macOS systems, making it a good choice if you're sharing files between different types of computers. **Important Note:** Formatting a drive will delete all existing data on the drive. Make sure to back up any important files before proceeding. ### Step 1: Connect the Drive to Your Mac First, connect the drive you want to format to your Mac. You can do this using a USB cable, Thunderbolt cable, or any other appropriate connection method for your specific drive. Once connected, your Mac should recognize the drive and mount it on your desktop or within the Finder sidebar under the "Locations" section. Connecting the drive allows your Mac to communicate with the storage device and prepare it for formatting. ### Step 2: Open Disk Utility Next, open Disk Utility, which is an application built into macOS that manages disks and volumes. To do so, press `Cmd` + `Space` to open Spotlight search, type "Disk Utility," then hit `Enter`. Alternatively, you can find Disk Utility within the Utilities folder inside the Applications folder. Using Disk Utility provides you with a user-friendly interface for managing and modifying drives and partitions. ### Step 3: Select the Drive to Format In Disk Utility, locate the drive you wish to format in the left sidebar. It may appear as its brand name (e.g., SanDisk, Western Digital) or simply as "Untitled." If you are unsure about which drive to select, check the capacity listed next to each item to ensure you choose the correct one. Selecting the correct drive ensures that you don't accidentally wipe valuable data from another drive. ### Step 4: Erase the Drive With the correct drive selected, click the "Erase" button at the top of the Disk Utility window. A popup window will appear asking you to enter some information regarding the format settings. * **Name:** Enter a descriptive name for the drive, such as "MyFat32Drive." * **Format:** Choose "MS-DOS (FAT)" from the dropdown menu to set the format to FAT32. * **Scheme:** Leave this option set to "Master Boot Record" unless you have a specific reason to change it. Click the "Erase" button once you've entered the required information. Disk Utility will now format the drive as FAT32. Setting the format to FAT32 makes the drive compatible with both Windows and macOS systems. ### Step 5: Eject the Drive Once the formatting process has completed, eject the drive safely by clicking the eject icon next to the drive's name in the Disk Utility sidebar or by right-clicking the drive icon on your desktop or within the Finder sidebar and selecting "Eject." Properly ejecting the drive prevents potential data corruption issues. Ejecting the drive protects your data and maintains the integrity of the formatted drive. Congratulations! You have successfully formatted a FAT32 drive using your Mac. The drive is now ready for use with either Windows or macOS systems.
