Title: How to Access Criminal Court Records Introduction: Criminal court records contain valuable information about criminal cases that have been processed through the judicial system. These records can be used for various purposes such as background checks, research, or personal interest. However, accessing these records is not always straightforward due to legal restrictions and privacy concerns. This guide will walk you through the process of accessing criminal court records while highlighting important considerations along the way. Step 1: Identify the Court Where the Case Was Heard Determine which court heard the case you're interested in. This could be a municipal court, state trial court, appellate court, or even federal court depending on the nature of the crime and its jurisdiction. Knowing the correct court makes your search more efficient. Key Tip: If unsure where the case was tried, try searching online databases like PACER (Public Access to Court Electronic Records) for federal courts or contact the clerk of the court in the county where the alleged crime occurred. Step 2: Understand Public Access Policies Each court has its own policies regarding public access to records. Some allow full access, some limit what information can be viewed, and others may require permission from the individuals involved before releasing any details. Familiarize yourself with these rules to avoid unnecessary obstacles during your search. Guideline: Be respectful when requesting record access. Remember that courthouses are sensitive places handling confidential matters. Step 3: Locate the Record Room or Office Once at the appropriate courthouse, locate the record room or office responsible for managing and distributing court documents. This is typically found near the main entrance or inquiry desk. Key Tip: Don't hesitate to ask staff members if you need help finding this area. Step 4: Conduct Your Search There are several ways to conduct your search once you reach the record room or office. You might be able to look up the record using the name of one of the parties involved, the case number, or the date of the incident. Depending on the court, this process may involve manual paper files, computer terminals, or both. Guideline: Keep track of all the details related to your search. Having accurate information streamlines the process and reduces potential errors. Step 5: Review the Record After locating the relevant record, take time to review it carefully. Make sure it pertains to the correct case and individual(s). Also, note that redactions may occur to protect certain types of private information. Key Tip: Bring a notebook and pen to jot down notes or copy relevant sections. However, do not remove pages or alter original documents. Step 6: Request Copies (if Necessary) If you want physical copies of the record, most courts charge fees for reproduction services. Pay these costs as required and ensure you receive official certified copies if needed. Guideline: Always retain proof of payment and receipt of copied records. Conclusion: Accessing criminal court records requires patience and knowledge of specific procedures. By following this comprehensive guide, you increase chances of successfully obtaining necessary information while respecting legal boundaries and ethical norms. I know it was back there, wasn't it? It was the thought that counts, right? You must have gone to a beach with daddy and Louise. He was footin' it along the road ahead of us. [reaches into mailbox, pretends to hand something to MOT] Bullying and the Bystander Bullying usually involves three individuals or groups: the bully, the victim and the bystander. Bullying is repeated, aggressive behaviour towards one person from another person or group of people--and it is hurtful. It might be shoving, name-calling, lunch-stealing or even gossiping. You may think that as long as you're not the bully you're not hurting anyone, but that's not true--the bystander plays an important role in a bullying situation. YTV believes that bystanders have the power to help stop bullying. - Bystanders (other kids watching) are present most of the time (85%) when there is a bully episode on the playground or in the classroom. - Most kids feel uncomfortable witnessing bullying, but very few intervene. - When peers step in, the bullying stops within ten seconds 57% of the time. HOW BYSTANDERS CAN HELP You're a "bystander" if you watch the bullying, but don't take part in it. In fact, you are exactly the audience that the bully wants. But you can help stop the bullying. Here are some tips: - Recognize bullying -- It's not just physical. It can be social and verbal as well. Telling someone they're "ugly" is considered bullying, and can be just as hurtful as hitting them. - Walk away -- By standing around and watching you encourage the bully. Walk away and go get help. - Encourage bystanders to get involved as a group -- Band together and walk away. - Keep track of places where bullying is taking place -- If you tell an adult in charge, they will monitor these areas more closely. - Speak up -- Tell the bully that they're wrong and that you won't get involved in any bullying. - Help the victim -- Put yourself in their shoes. Would you want someone to help you if you were being picked on? Of course! THERE ARE NO INNOCENT BYSTANDERS If you're standing around watching, you're part of the problem not the solution. NOTE: Reporting bullying is NOT tattling. Tattling is when you want to get someone in trouble. When you report bullying, you are helping someone else. It is important to know and understand the difference. IT'S DIFFERENT FOR BOYS AND GIRLS... If you find yourself in a situation where you are being pressured into doing something you don't want to do, try these tools. Boys and girls have different experiences with bullying, so we have some tips for both of you. - STOP and THINK. Don't just rush in. - Walk away. - Make a joke, sometimes humour is the best way to relieve tension. - Let it pass, ignore the situation and carry on as if nothing happened. - Talk it out. It can be a teacher or a friend. If something is really bothering you, talk about it. - Create a scene to draw attention away from the bullying. Yell, sing, jump around, anything that will draw people away from the bully. - Speak up! You have a right to be heard. - Have a positive attitude. - Make up your own mind. Just because everyone else is doing it, doesn't mean you have to as well. - Choose your friends carefully. Like them for their good qualities, not just because they're popular. - Be true to yourself. In the end, no one matters more than you do. Don't put up with bullying in your school! ARE YOU A VICTIM OF BULLYING? Bullying is scary and embarrassing. It can make you feel as if it's your fault--it's not! Here are a few things to remember: - THIS WILL END. You will not have to feel this bad forever!! - Stay calm, bullies LOVE a reaction so don't give them one. - Don't fight back; you may get hurt or make the situation worse. - Try to calmly withdraw from the situation. - Avoid being alone. - Try to use humour to diffuse the situation. - Don't be afraid to tell an adult you trust. Even if you just want to talk about it, they will listen. SOURCES FOR HELP WITH BULLYING Talk to an adult you trust, at home or at school. If you've tried the above tips and the bullying still continues, then check out these other sources for more help: To talk to someone try Kids Help Phone Whatever the problem, Kids Help Phone is available 24/7 to help you. They offer free confidential counselling and information on the phone or online. You can reach them at 1-800-668-6868 or post a message online at kidshelpphone.ca For information try: Concerned Children's Advertisers -- they have loads more info on bullying. Visit www.cca-kids.ca You should know how to do that. "There's one thing more. I just wanted to say that if--if you are ever in trouble, anything that I can do--" He took her hand in his, held it tightly for a minute, then dropped it and walked rapidly away towards the Casino without looking back. Backward Long Jump in Super Mario 64: A Comprehensive Tutorial Super Mario 64 is a classic 3D platformer game that offers many challenging moves and techniques for players to master. One such technique is the backward long jump, which can help you cover large distances quickly and reach otherwise inaccessible areas. This tutorial will provide an in-depth explanation of how to perform this advanced maneuver with precision and consistency. Step 1: Basic Controls Familiarization Before attempting the backward long jump, ensure you are comfortable with the basic controls of Super Mario 64: * Analog stick (or D-pad): Move Mario around the environment * 'A' button: Jump * 'B' button: Run or dive (when in mid-air) Step 2: Entering the Crouch Position To initiate the backward long jump, first, position Mario so he faces away from your intended jumping direction. Then press down on the analog stick or crouch using the 'Z' button if you are using a GameCube controller. Hold the crouch position briefly; releasing too early may result in a regular jump instead. Key Tip: Timing is crucial when entering the crouch position. Practice pressing the crouch button just before reaching the peak of your running animation to maintain momentum during the jump. Step 3: Initiating the Jump While still holding the crouch position, press the 'A' button to leap backward. Be sure not to hold the 'A' button down for too long, as doing so might trigger a triple jump, wasting valuable distance. Instead, tap the button quickly and release it immediately after Mario leaves the ground. Guideline: The ideal jump height should be slightly higher than a standard standing jump but lower than a full triple jump. Experiment with different timings until you find what works best for you. Step 4: Performing the Mid-Air Turn As soon as Mario jumps backward, turn him around by rapidly flicking the analog stick in the opposite direction or tapping the 'B' button twice while airborne. It's essential to execute this step swiftly since delaying the turn could cause you to lose altitude and fall short of your target destination. Key Tips: * Use small flicks or taps rather than large movements to minimize turning time. * If you struggle with precise turns, consider practicing in a controlled environment like Princess Peach's Castle courtyard before attempting more challenging jumps. Step 5: Extending the Jump Distance After executing the mid-air turn, extend both arms outward by holding either left ('L') or right ('R') shoulder buttons. Keeping them pressed throughout the remainder of the jump grants extra lift and propels Mario farther forward. Remember that excessive arm extension might lead to losing balance upon landing, causing potential damage if there isn't enough clearance beneath you. Step 6: Sticking the Landing Finally, prepare for touchdown by bending your knees slightly just before making contact with the surface. Upon landing safely, release all buttons except those needed for movement control. Congratulations! You have successfully executed a backward long jump in Super Mario 64. Remember that practice makes perfect - don't be discouraged if initial attempts fail. With persistence and dedication, mastering the backward long jump will become second nature, opening up new possibilities within the expansive world of Super Mario 64. " Did you fly in the airplane? Did you get all the animals out? Title: How to Become a Cool Person Introduction: Being cool isn't about following trends or trying to impress others. It's about being comfortable with who you are, having confidence in yourself, and treating people with kindness and respect. This guide will provide you with actionable steps and insights on how to become a genuinely cool person. Remember, authenticity is essential; don't try to be someone you're not. Step 1: Embrace Your Individuality * Explanation: Being unique is one of the most attractive qualities a person can have. Instead of trying to fit in with the crowd, celebrate what makes you different. * Key Tips: + Identify your strengths, passions, and interests. + Don't shy away from sharing your thoughts and opinions (respectfully). + Surround yourself with positive influences that appreciate and encourage your individuality. Step 2: Develop Genuine Confidence * Explanation: Confident individuals command respect and admiration from those around them. However, this doesn't mean boasting or putting others down. Open the Cash App on your Android device: The first step is to locate and open the Cash App icon on your home screen or app drawer. This will take you to the main page of the app where you can access all its features. 2. Navigate to the Activity tab: Once you have opened the Cash App, look for the "Activity" tab at the bottom of the screen. Tap on this tab to view a list of all your recent transactions. 3. Find the transaction you want to cancel: Scroll through the list of transactions until you find the one you wish to cancel. Once you've located it, tap on it to view more details about the transaction. 4. Check if the transaction is eligible for cancellation: Before attempting to cancel a transaction, check if it is still pending or has already been processed. If the transaction has already been completed, then unfortunately, it cannot be cancelled. However, if the transaction is still pending, you may be able to cancel it. 5. Tap the "..." button: Next to the transaction details, there should be three dots ("...") arranged vertically. Tapping on this button will bring up additional options related to the transaction. 6. Select "Cancel Payment": From the dropdown menu that appears after tapping the "..." button, select the option labeled "Cancel Payment." This will initiate the cancellation process for the selected transaction. 7. Confirm the cancellation: After selecting "Cancel Payment," a confirmation message will appear asking if you are sure you want to cancel the payment. Make sure you double-check the amount and recipient before confirming the cancellation. 8. Wait for the funds to return: Once the cancellation has been confirmed, the funds should automatically be returned to your account within 10 business days. You can monitor the status of the cancellation by checking the activity feed in the Cash App. 9. Contact Support (if necessary): If the funds do not return to your account within 10 business days, or if you encounter any issues during the cancellation process, contact Cash App support for further assistance. They can help troubleshoot any problems and ensure that the funds are returned to your account as soon as possible. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Be aware that some transactions may not be cancellable. For example, transactions that have already been processed or authorized by the recipient may not be eligible for cancellation. * Always double-check the recipient's information before sending a payment. Typos or errors in the recipient's email address or phone number could result in the payment being sent to the wrong person. * Keep track of your transaction history in the Cash App. Regularly monitoring your account activity can help you identify potential fraud or unauthorized charges, which can then be reported to Cash App support. * Use caution when sharing personal or financial information with anyone online or over the phone. Never share your Cash App login credentials or QR code with anyone, even if they claim to be from Cash App support. * Finally, always make sure you are using the latest version of the Cash App. Updating the app regularly can help protect against security vulnerabilities and improve overall functionality. The next natural number can be found by adding 1 to the current natural number, producing numbers that go on "forever". There is no natural number that is infinite in size. There are nine essential amino acids for humans, which are obtained from food. The nine essential amino acids are: histidine, isoleucine, leucine, lysine, methionine, phenylalanine, threonine, tryptophan, and valine. Meat contains all the essential amino acids humans need; most plants do not. However, eating a mixture of plants, such as both wheat "and" peanut butter, or rice "and" beans, provides all the essential amino acids needed. Soy products like tofu provide all the essential amino acids-as does quinoa-but these are not the only way to get the protein humans need. Plans or Planes? By: Alea Kent Rationale: In order for children to become successful readers, they must learn to decode. The skills children need to decode include recognizing and understanding grapheme to phoneme correspondences. The goal of this lesson is to teach children the grapheme A or a and its corresponding phoneme a_e= /A/. They will do this by participating in several activities including listening to the phoneme a_e=/A/, saying the phoneme in a tongue twister, reading and spelling words with a_e=/A/ in a letter box lesson, and reading a decodable book, Jane and Babe. -long strip of poster with tongue twister: Amy waves "AAAA!" to her friends while she bakes cakes all day to trade. -bag for each student with Elkonin letter boxes and letters (a, t, e, c, g, s, k, p, l, r, m, b) - copy of book, Jane and Babe, for each student -worksheet for each student (attached) - pieces of paper with letterbox lesson words written on them: at, ate, ace, age, ask, plate, grape, camp, blame, stare 1. Review a=/a/. "Can anyone tell me what sound short a makes? That's right, it makes the /a/ sound. Let's see if you can identify some words with the a=/a/ sound. Do you hear /a/ in cat or dog? Ask or tell? Rag or cloth? That or then? Slam or shut? 2. Great job! Now we are going learn another sound that the letter a can make. When the letter a is in a word followed by a consonant and e at the end, it says /A/. Can everyone say /A/ with me? It is almost like when people wave to each other and say 'AAAAA!' Can everyone wave to each other and say 'AAAA!'" 3. "Now we are going to do a tongue twister." Place strip with tongue twister on board so the children can read along as they say it. "Amy waves "AAAA!" to her friends while she bakes cakes all day to trade." First, the teacher should say it once to the students. Once this is done, ask the students to say it aloud with you at normal speed. Once they say it at normal speed, make the /A/ sounds longer in each word. 4. Begin letterbox lesson. "Now we are going to practice reading and writing a few words with the letter a." Pass out bags with Elkonin letterboxes and letters. Model how to spell and read a word. "Now I am going to show you how to spell a word using our boxes. I am going to spell scrap. It has five sounds, so I will need five boxes; /s/ /c/ /r/ /a/ /p/, scrap. I put the first sound, /s/, in the first box. The next sound I hear is /c/, so I will put it in the next box. The next sound I hear is /r/, so it will go in the third box. The fourth sound I hear is /a/, so it goes in the fourth box. The last sound I hear is /p/, so it goes in the last box. Now I am going to spell scrape, it still just has five sounds so I will still need five boxes; /s/ /c/ /r/ /A/ /p/. I just put my e on the outside of the boxes because I know it takes an e to make the letter a say /A/. Now I am going to read two words." Write plan on the board. "This word says plan, /p/ /l/ /a/ /n/, plan. Now if I add an e to the end of the word, it changes how I say it. Now I say it /p/ /l/ /A/ /n/, plane. Now it is your turn to try." 5. The children will review some /a/ words as well as practice with the new phoneme /A/. "Now you may take out your letterboxes and letters. I will call out a word for you to spell. Then I will use the word in a sentence so you will understand the meaning of the word." Listed below is the list of words to ask the children to spell. As you are calling out the words, walk around the room observing the children and helping students who need it. 2: at, ate, ace, age 4: plate, grape, camp, blame, stare 6. Once they are finished spelling the words, it is now time to read them. Have each letterbox word written on a piece of paper to hold up. Ask the children to read each word they have just spelled out loud as a group. 7. "Since you all have done so well learning that 'a' can sometimes say /A/, we are now going to read a book called Jane and Babe. In this book, Jane has a friend who is unlike most of her other friends. He is a lion named Babe. Babe lives in a cage, but because Babe is tame, Jane will often go into his cage to play with him. One day, Jane cannot wake Babe up. You'll have to read to find out if she finally wakes him up. I hope he isn't angry!" Place students in pairs for them to read to each other. Instruct them to help each other if they need it. If they still need help, they can raise their hand and you will assist them. 8. Once the children have finished reading, they may go back to their seats and begin the assessment worksheet. (see attached) "Now we are going to do one final activity for this lesson. I am going to pass out a worksheet that I want you to do your best on. On the worksheet are several pictures. Under each picture, I want you to write the word that describes what the picture is. Other times, there will be a word, and I want you to draw a picture of the word." Do the first picture on the worksheet as an example for them. Cushman, Sheila, & Rona Kornblum. Jane and Babe. 1990. Understanding Melodies: Intervals: Half Steps and Whole Steps There are two different kinds of step: the half step and the whole step. Understanding these intervals is extremely important to accurately create scales and chords. The half step is the distance from one pitch to the next possible pitch. Half steps are very easy to see when we use the piano keyboard like the one introduced in the section on sound. When you point to any key, the name of that pitch will become visible. If you are unable to view the keyboard, download the latest version of Flash Player at www.Adobe.com. Find the pitch G on the keyboard, then point at the next possible pitch to the right of G (in this case a black key). It shows us that a half step above G is either called G sharp or A flat. To determine a half step below G, go through the same process but point to the pitch to left of G, which is called either G flat or F sharp. Remember that when one note has two different names, they are called enharmonic equivalents. A whole step is defined as two half steps. Avoid the temptation to think of a whole step as one letter name to the next letter name. For example, look at the pitches E and F on the keyboard. There is no black key between them, so the interval between E and F is a half step. To determine the whole step above E, count two half steps to the right of that key. The whole step above E is F sharp. Oh [whine] I don't know the name of it but... You mean people that rob your house? Because of where they live (and unlike many animals), whales are conscious breathers: they decide when to breathe. Whales breathe through blowholes. Baleen whales have two and toothed whales have one. These are on the top of the head: the animal breathes while most of their body is underwater. Breathing first shoots out extra water from the blowhole, making a jet into the air, followed by inhaling air into the lungs. London is the city region with the highest population in the United Kingdom. With it being located along River Thames, London has been a central city since it was founded by the Romans two millennia ago under the name Londinium. London's original city center, the City of London, which in 2011 had 7,375 inhabitants on an area of 2.9 km , is England's smallest city. Since the end of the 19th century, London has also been used for the urban region that has developed around this city center. This area forms the region of London, as well as the Greater London administrative unit, led by the Mayor of London and the London Assembly. In modern times, London is one of the world's most important political, economic and cultural centers. This is not least due to the fact that London was the capital of the British Empire and thus for almost three centuries the center of power for large parts of the globe. Title: How to Achieve Perfectly Browned Potatoes Introduction: Crispy, golden-brown potatoes are a delightful addition to any meal. This tutorial will guide you through the process of achieving perfectly browned potatoes with clear, concise steps and helpful tips along the way. Whether you're making home fries, roasted potatoes, or a breakfast hash, this method will ensure even browning and delicious results every time. Materials Needed: * Potatoes (preferably Russet or Yukon Gold) * Olive oil or vegetable oil * Salt * Pepper * Paper towels * Baking sheet or skillet * Spatula or tongs Step 1: Choose Your Potatoes Select either Russet or Yukon Gold potatoes as they have higher starch content, which contributes to better browning. Avoid waxy varieties like Red Bliss or New Potatoes, as they tend to steam rather than brown. Step 2: Prepare the Potatoes Wash the potatoes thoroughly under running water to remove dirt and debris. Cut the potatoes into your preferred shape - cubes, slices, wedges, etc. The smaller the pieces, the faster they will cook and brown. Make sure all cuts are similar in size to promote even cooking. Key Tip: Soak cut potatoes in cold water for at least 30 minutes to an hour to release excess starch. This prevents sticking during cooking and promotes crispier potatoes. After soaking, drain well and pat dry using paper towels before proceeding to Step 4. Step 3: Preheat the Oven or Skillet Preheating is crucial for proper browning. If baking, preheat your oven to 425 F (220 C). For stove top cooking, heat a non-stick or cast iron skillet over medium-high heat until hot but not smoking. Step 4: Season the Potatoes Toss the dried potato pieces with enough oil to coat them lightly but evenly. Add salt and pepper according to taste. Mix well to distribute seasonings uniformly. Key Guideline: Overcrowding leads to steaming instead of browning. Work in batches if necessary to avoid crowding the pan or baking sheet. Step 5: Arrange the Potatoes Spread out the oiled and seasoned potato pieces onto the heated baking sheet or skillet in a single layer without overlapping. Step 6: Cook the Potatoes For baked potatoes, place the tray in the preheated oven and cook for about 20-30 minutes, flipping once halfway through, until both sides are nicely browned and crispy. On the stovetop, let the potatoes sizzle undisturbed for several minutes (about 5 minutes per side), then turn them over using a spatula or tongs to brown another side. Continue turning occasionally until desired doneness is reached. Key Tips: * Resist moving the potatoes around too much; allow them to sear properly to develop color. * Use high smoke-point oils such as grapeseed, canola, or peanut oil to prevent burning. Step 7: Serve Hot Once cooked to perfection, immediately transfer the browned potatoes to serving dishes. Enjoy while warm! Conclusion: With these simple yet effective techniques, you now possess the knowledge required to create consistently excellent browned potatoes. Remember that patience, correct preparation, and attentiveness yield superior results. Happy cooking! Jamie Farr (born July 1, 1934) is an American actor of Lebanese descent. He is best known for his role as Corp./later Sgt. Maxwell Q. Klinger on the 1970s TV show, "M*A*S*H". His real name is Jameel Joseph Farha and he was born in Toledo, Ohio. The barons invited a French invasion by Prince Louis VIII of France and Louis accepted the offer of the crown of England as a reward for his support. [note text broken up to prevent error] Title: How to Perform a Hard Reset on an iPod Touch A hard reset can be a useful troubleshooting step when your iPod Touch is experiencing issues such as freezing, crashing, or not responding. This process will restart your device and should resolve any minor software glitches. It's important to note that this procedure won't delete any data from your iPod Touch; however, we recommend backing up your data regularly as a precautionary measure. Before proceeding with the hard reset, ensure that your iPod Touch has at least 30% battery life remaining to prevent unexpected shutdown during the process. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** - A hard reset does NOT erase any data from your iPod Touch. - Make sure your device has enough battery power before starting. - Regularly back up your data as a safety measure. - If you cannot perform these steps due to an unresponsive screen, try connecting the iPod Touch to a computer using its USB cable and then attempt the following steps again. Here are the detailed instructions to do a hard reset on an iPod Touch: 1. **Locate the Sleep/Wake Button.** The Sleep/Wake button is located on the top edge of your iPod Touch. This button is used to turn off and lock your device. 2. **Find the Home Button.** The Home button is found at the bottom center part of your iPod Touch's front face. Pressing this button returns you to the home screen from wherever you are within the interface. *These two buttons will be essential throughout the entire hard reset process.* 3. **Press and Hold the Sleep/Wake Button.** With both fingers ready over their respective buttons, press and hold down the Sleep/Wake button until the "slide to power off" slider appears on the screen. 4. **Drag the Slider to Power Off Your Device.** Release the Sleep/Wake button once the slider appears. Now, drag the slider from left to right across the screen. Wait approximately 5 seconds for your iPod Touch to completely shut off after the slider disappears from view. *This action turns off your iPod Touch, which is necessary prior to performing the actual hard reset.* 5. **Hold Down Both Buttons Simultaneously.** After waiting about 5 seconds since turning off your device, press and hold BOTH the Sleep/Wake button AND the Home button together. Continue holding them even if the Apple logo briefly flashes on the screen - don't release yet! 6. **Release When You See the Apple Logo Again.** Keep holding both buttons until you see the white Apple logo reappear. Only once the Apple logo shows up again, should you let go of both buttons simultaneously. 7. **Wait for Initialization Process Completion.** After releasing the buttons, allow some time for your iPod Touch to fully initialize (about 30 seconds). During this period, you'll notice a progress wheel spinning beneath the Apple logo. Once initialization finishes, your Lock Screen will display, indicating that your iPod Touch has successfully completed the hard reset process. 8. **Confirm Restoration by Checking Functionality.** To confirm whether the hard reset resolved your previous issue(s), test out various functionalities like scrolling through apps, playing music, browsing online, etc. If problems persist, consider seeking further assistance via official Apple Support channels. Congratulations! By completing this guide, you have now learned how to effectively execute a hard reset on your iPod Touch! Title: How to Get Over a Loved One Going on Vacation ===================================================== Going on vacation is an exciting time, but if your loved one is leaving you behind, it can be difficult to cope with their absence. You may experience feelings of loneliness, sadness, or even jealousy. However, there are several steps you can take to help ease these emotions and maintain a healthy relationship while they're away. This wikiHow will guide you through this process. ### Step 1: Acknowledge Your Feelings *Estimated Time Needed: 5-10 minutes* Before your loved one departs, acknowledge any negative emotions that arise due to their upcoming trip. Recognizing these feelings early on will allow you to address them effectively before they escalate into more significant issues during their absence. #### Key Tips & Guidelines * Identify specific emotions such as fear, anxiety, sadness, anger, or frustration. * Understand that experiencing these emotions is normal when dealing with separation from a loved one. * Allow yourself to feel the emotion without judgment; validate its presence rather than suppressing it. ### Step 2: Communicate Openly With Your Loved One *Estimated Time Needed: 15-30 minutes* Discuss your concerns and fears about being separated with your loved one openly. Honest communication fosters understanding and strengthens relationships. It also enables both parties to find solutions together. #### Key Tips & Guidelines * Share your thoughts and worries calmly and respectfully. What You Should Know "Tick Talk" Video "Bright White" PSA from CA Department of Public Health Ticks are blood-eating parasites that live and feed on mammals, birds and reptiles around the world. There are about 850 different types of ticks. There are two families, the soft and the hard. Ticks have a rough structure which is used to break the skin of their host in order to feed on their blood. This rough structure has many beak-like projections on it. They have backward projections that make it hard to remove them from their host. Some types of ticks also create a cement-like substance which actually glues the tick to the host while it is feeding. After the tick is done, the substance dissolves. Ticks look for hosts by what is called "questing". Questing ticks crawl up stems of grass or perch on the edges of leaves on the ground with their front legs extended. When a potential host passes by their extended legs, the ticks climb onto them. Ticks feed for different amounts of time on their hosts, varying from a couple of hours to weeks, depending on the type of host and type of tick. Ticks are known carries of diseases such as Lyme Disease and Tularemia. Click here to learn all about Lyme Disease. Click here to learn all about Tularemia. Tick Life Cycle - Egg: Adult female ticks lay eggs on the ground in the spring. - Larva: In the summer, the eggs hatch into larvae. Then they attach to a host, begin feeding and over a few days, swell up with blood. Larvae feed on small mammals, deer and birds in the late summer and early fall. - Nymph: After feeding, most larvae drop off their hosts and transform into nymphs in the fall. They stay there until the next spring. Nymphs then attach to their host and feed for 4 to 5 days, while swelling with blood. Nymphs feed on rodents, small mammals, birds and humans in the spring and summer. - Adult: Once swollen, nymphs drop off their host and molts into an adult. Adult ticks seek new hosts in the fall, waiting up to 3 feet above the ground on the tips of grasses and shrubs, often along trails. Ticks do not jump or fly. Adult ticks feed on deer, humans, dogs, cats, horses and other animals. Show her how well you can draw. [takes the cup down and looks at MOT] But you height sit in between us. Title: How to Charge a New DELL Inspiron 6000 Battery Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of charging a new battery for your DELL Inspiron 6000 laptop. Properly charging your new battery is crucial to ensure its longevity and optimal performance. This guide assumes that you have already installed the battery in your laptop. If you haven't, please refer to your user manual or DELL's official website for installation instructions before proceeding with this tutorial. Step 1: Plug in Your Laptop Connect the AC adapter (power cord) to an electrical outlet and plug the other end into your laptop. Make sure the connection between the power cord and the laptop is secure to avoid any accidental disconnection during the charging process. Key Tip: Always use the original AC adapter provided by DELL for your specific model or a compatible one from a reputable source. Using an incorrect charger may damage your battery or even your laptop itself. Step 2: Turn On Your Laptop Press the power button located at the front or side of your laptop to turn it on. You should see the DELL logo appear followed by the Windows login screen if your system has already been set up. Step 3: Check the Power Indicator Light Once your laptop is turned on, look for the power indicator light which is typically found near the power button or along the edge of the laptop where the battery resides. The color of the light may vary depending on the model; however, when charging, it usually turns orange, yellow, or amber. Step 4: Monitor the Battery Icon On the taskbar at the bottom right corner of your screen, locate the battery icon. It looks like a battery with several bars indicating its charge level. When connected to the power adapter, it should show a lightning bolt symbol next to it signifying that it is currently charging. Step 5: Allow Full Charging Cycle Let the battery charge until the indicator light remains solid instead of blinking, and the battery icon shows 100% charged. A full charging cycle means using the battery down to about 5-10%, then fully recharging it back to 100%. For brand new batteries, though, simply charging it to 100% once without draining can also be considered as completing a charging cycle. Guideline: According to Battery University, performing at least two to three full charging cycles within the first few weeks after purchasing a new battery can help calibrate the battery gauge, providing more accurate readings throughout its lifespan. Step 6: Unplug After Fully Charged After reaching 100% charge, unplug the power cord from both the wall socket and the laptop. Continuing to keep the battery plugged in after being fully charged might degrade its capacity over time due to overcharging. However, modern laptops including DELL models employ smart battery systems designed to prevent such issues. Nevertheless, occasional deep cycling - running the laptop solely on battery power till it shuts off automatically due to low battery - is recommended every couple of months for maintaining overall battery health. Conclusion: Now you know how to properly charge your new DELL Inspiron 6000 battery! Following these steps ensures better battery life and improved efficiency. Remember to occasionally check the manufacturer's recommendations regarding maintenance and care for updates since technology evolves continuously. Happy computing! Einstein's general theory of relativity uses the idea of spacetime. Spacetime is the fact that we have a four-dimensional universe, having three spatial (space) dimensions and one temporal (time) dimension. Any physical event happens at some place inside these three space dimensions, and at some moment in time. According to the general theory of relativity, any mass causes spacetime to curve, and any other mass follows these curves. Bigger mass causes more curving. This was a new way to explain gravitation (gravity). = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Parent Child/SLI/PC-ac/bla15.cha = = = [2 sec silence, then S speaks just a bit ahead of D] "Gillette!" he cried, "let us go. He may put them in the mouth. Title: How to Avoid Using Cruel Words Introduction: Using cruel words can harm others and damage relationships. By avoiding such language, you can create a more positive environment and foster better connections with people around you. This guide will provide you with detailed steps on how to avoid using cruel words, along with helpful tips and guidelines to ensure clarity and practical application. Step 1: Identify Triggering Situations Determine what situations or emotions tend to make you use cruel words. Common triggers include anger, frustration, stress, and jealousy. Understanding your personal triggers is essential to managing your reactions effectively. * Key Tip: Keep a journal to record instances where you've used cruel words. Note down the situation, your emotional state, and any potential triggers that may have contributed to your behavior. Over time, this practice will help you identify patterns and become more aware of your actions. Step 2: Practice Self-Regulation Develop strategies to manage your emotions when triggered. Deep breathing exercises, meditation, and mindfulness techniques can all be effective methods for regulating your feelings and preventing impulsive outbursts. * Guideline: Set aside five minutes daily to practice relaxation techniques, even if you aren't currently feeling stressed or angry. Consistent practice will make these skills second nature, enabling you to access them quickly during challenging moments. Step 3: Rethink Your Thoughts Before speaking, take a moment to evaluate your thoughts. Are they kind, considerate, and empathetic? If not, reframe them in a way that promotes positivity and understanding. * Key Tip: Use the "ABCDE" model developed by psychologist Albert Ellis. A represents adversity (the trigger), B stands for beliefs (your initial reaction), C denotes consequences (how your belief affects your behavior), D signifies disputation (challenging your negative thought pattern), and E refers to energization (replacing negativity with healthier alternatives). Step 4: Choose Empathetic Language Opt for words that demonstrate compassion and respect rather than resorting to harshness or rudeness. Consider how your choice of vocabulary might impact the person you are addressing. * Guideline: When communicating, imagine yourself as the recipient of your own words. Would you appreciate being spoken to in such a manner? If not, adjust your language accordingly. Step 5: Seek Alternatives Instead of using cruel words, find alternative ways to express your frustrations or concerns without causing harm. For instance, instead of saying "You never listen to me!", try something like, "I feel unheard when we discuss important matters." * Key Tip: Utilize assertiveness training resources, which teach communication skills centered around self-expression while still considering others' needs and feelings. Step 6: Apologize Sincerely If you do slip up and use cruel words despite your best efforts, offer a genuine apology. Express remorse, acknowledge the hurt caused, and commit to making amends. * Guideline: Offer an authentic apology that includes specific details about the offense and demonstrates responsibility for your actions. Refrain from including excuses or justifications within your statement. Conclusion: By following these steps, you can significantly reduce the usage of cruel words in your interactions. Remember that learning new habits takes time and patience; don't be too hard on yourself if progress seems slow at first. With consistent effort, you can cultivate a more empathic and compassionate approach to communication. That's a pretty neat thing to do. You going to sing happy birthday to your cake? Title: How to Grow a Lucky Bamboo Plant Lucky bamboo (Dracaena sanderiana) is an attractive, low-maintenance plant that brings good fortune according to Feng Shui principles. It's actually not a true bamboo but rather a member of the Dracaena family. This plant can thrive in various environments, including homes, offices, and greenhouses. With proper care and attention, you will be able to grow and maintain a healthy, vibrant lucky bamboo plant. Here are the steps to guide you through this process. ### Step 1: Choosing Your Lucky Bamboo Plant Buy a healthy, young lucky bamboo plant from a reputable nursery or online store. Look for plants with bright green leaves and straight stems. Avoid those with yellowing or brown leaves as they may indicate poor health or improper care. Choose a stalk with at least three joints, which symbolize happiness, wealth, and longevity. The more joints, the better! Key Tips & Guidelines: * Select a stalk that fits your desired size; smaller stalks will grow taller over time while larger stalks remain shorter. * Make sure the roots are white and firm without any signs of rot or decay. ### Step 2: Preparation Prepare a clean vase or container for your lucky bamboo plant. A clear glass vase allows easy monitoring of water levels and root growth. However, other materials like ceramic or plastic containers work fine too. Decorate the vase if desired using pebbles, marbles, or colored stones for added aesthetics. These additions also provide support for the bamboo stem. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Use a container deep enough to accommodate about one inch of water above the rocks or marbles. * Rinse decorative elements before placing them into the container to remove dust and debris. ### Step 3: Placement and Arrangement Place your lucky bamboo plant in an area with indirect sunlight. Direct exposure can scorch its leaves, leading to discoloration and weakened growth. Filtered light from nearby windows or artificial lighting works best. Arrange the stalk(s) artistically within the container, ensuring stability by positioning them between the rocks or marbles. Key Tips & Guidelines: An' catch you an' bounce an' catch you. Title: How to Get Silvery Blonde Hair Silver blonde hair is a stunning and unique shade that can make you stand out from the crowd. However, achieving this color requires careful planning, patience, and knowledge about hair care. This tutorial will provide an in-depth guide on how to get silver blonde hair while minimizing damage to your locks. **Step 1: Consult a Professional Colorist** Before embarking on your journey towards silvery blonde hair, consult with a professional colorist who has experience in bleaching and dying hair to this shade. They'll assess your current hair condition, discuss any potential risks or challenges, and give advice on aftercare. Key Tip: Bring pictures of the exact shade of silver blonde you want to help communicate your goal clearly. **Step 2: Prepare Your Hair** Bleaching damages hair, so preparing yours beforehand can reduce harm. Use deep conditioning treatments once a week for at least a month prior to bleaching. Also, avoid using heat styling tools as much as possible during this period. Guideline: If your hair is already damaged (split ends, breakage), consider repairing it first before attempting to bleach it. **Step 3: Bleach Your Hair** This process should be done by a professional due to its complexity and risk of damaging your scalp and hair. The colorist will apply bleach evenly all over your head, focusing especially on darker areas. Depending on your natural hair color, you may need more than one session to reach the desired level of lightness. Key Tip: Never attempt to bleach your own hair unless you have extensive training; severe chemical burns can occur if not applied correctly. **Step 4: Tone Your Hair** After reaching the right level of lightness, your colorist will tone your hair to remove yellow undertones and create the cool, silvery hue. Toners usually contain purple pigments which counteract brassy tones. Expect multiple toning sessions since maintaining silver blonde often requires regular upkeep. Guideline: Always ask your colorist what products they used for bleaching and toning so you know what to purchase later for maintenance. **Step 5: Maintain Your New Color** To keep your silver blonde looking fresh, invest in quality shampoo and conditioner designed for colored hair. These products help prevent fading and maintain vibrancy longer. Additionally, try to wash your hair less frequently (around two times per week) to preserve the color. Key Tip: Look for products containing violet or purple pigments to neutralize brassiness between salon visits. **Step 6: Protect Your Hair** Use heat protectant sprays when styling to minimize damage caused by blow dryers, curlers, or straighteners. Regular trims are also essential to eliminate split ends and promote healthy growth. Guideline: Consider getting a haircut every six weeks to ensure optimal health and appearance of your silver blonde mane. Remember, going silver blonde is a significant change that demands time, money, and commitment. But with proper preparation, execution, and maintenance, you'll enjoy a fabulous new look! Free powerpoint Tutorials |home||Stay at Home and Learn| Create a Flow Chart in PowerPoint For slides five and six of your third presentation, you're going to design what's known as a flow chart. You've seen these before, no doubt. It's where you have a shape with a line or arrow leading to another shape. They are used to express ideas, logical chains, or organisational hierarchies. Here's the slide your going to be designing: This is a very simple Flow Chart and consists of three boxes and two arrows. If you run the presentation for this section again, watch what happens on slide 5. The first box appears and then, on a mouse click, the second box comes in from the left. After another mouse click, the third box arrives. The text at the bottom is not visible until the final mouse click. If you haven't quite grasped all that, don't worry - you'll see how it works as we build slide 5. So insert a new slide by clicking Insert > New Slide from the PowerPoint menu bar at the top. The tools we're going to be using are AutoShapes, and the line and arrow, in the image above. To add the rectangle, click on AutoShapes. A menu will appear. Title: How to Hit a Left-Handed Bunker Shot Introduction: Golfing from the sand can be intimidating, especially when you're left-handed. However, with proper technique and practice, you can master this shot. This tutorial will guide you through each step of hitting a successful left-handed bunker shot. Step 1: Equipment Selection Ensure your golf club is designed for lefties. A sand wedge is typically used due to its wide sole which glides along the sand rather than digging into it. The loft angle (usually between 54-58 degrees) launches the ball high into the air, helping it clear hazards. Step 2: Setting Up Place your feet shoulder-width apart, slightly open towards the target line. Your weight should favor your front foot by about 60%. Open the face of the sand wedge slightly; this increases the bounce angle, preventing digging into the sand. Key Tip: Don't overdo the clubface opening as it may lead to inconsistent shots or hooks. Step 3: Grip Hold the grip firmly but don't squeeze too hard. Use interlocking or overlapping grips according to what feels comfortable. Remember, your left hand controls direction while your right hand influences trajectory and distance control. Step 4: Ball Position Position the ball forward in your stance just inside your left heel. This encourages a descending strike on the sand, propelling the ball out efficiently. Step 5: Alignment Point your shoulders, hips, knees, and feet parallel to your intended target line. Visualize an imaginary extension of this line beyond the green where you want the ball to land. Step 6: Backswing Start your backswing by taking the club straight back without swaying your body laterally. Keep your arms close to your chest and maintain a steady head position. The shaft should reach vertical at the top of your swing. Step 7: Downswing & Impact Initiate the downswing with a rotation of your lower body followed by your upper body. Maintain speed throughout the swing, avoiding any abrupt stops. At impact, aim to hit one inch behind the ball, driving the clubhead under the ball. Key Guideline: Think 'explosion' - imagine blasting the sand away from beneath the ball. Step 8: Follow Through Complete the stroke naturally after the impact, letting your momentum carry the club upwards. Allow your wrists to uncock during this phase, keeping them flexible till the end of the swing. Step 9: Post-Shot Analysis After executing the shot, analyze the result. Did the ball fly far enough? Was it aimed correctly? What adjustments could improve future attempts? Conclusion: Practice makes perfect, so take time to hone these skills on the practice range before implementing them on the course. With patience and persistence, you'll soon become proficient at left-handed bunker shots! You have to get your other hand out of the way. [points to one of the little chairs] Won't he be mad?" Max laughed and said, "I can't tell you now. You have to come back on Wednesday and find out!" Lily was excited and couldn't wait for Wednesday to come. Chi don't talk back to me like that. Title: How to Eat Rambutan Rambutan is a tropical fruit that is native to Southeast Asia. It has a unique appearance with hairy red or yellow skin, and sweet white flesh inside. Eating rambutan can be a fun and delicious experience if you know how to do it properly. This tutorial will guide you through the process of eating rambutan, including important tips and guidelines to ensure clarity and practicality. **Step 1: Selecting Ripe Rambutans** Look for rambutans that are brightly colored and have firm but slightly soft skin. The hairs should also look fresh and not dry or brittle. Avoid rambutans that are brown or have mold on them as they may be overripe or spoiled. Key tip: Choose ripe rambutans that give slightly when pressed gently. Overripe ones may have an unpleasant taste and smell. **Step 2: Washing Rambutans** Before eating rambutans, wash them thoroughly under running water to remove any dirt or bacteria from their hairy exterior. Pat them dry with a clean towel. Guideline: Make sure to dry the rambutans completely before proceeding to the next step to prevent slipping while handling them. **Step 3: Removing the Skin** Hold the rambutan firmly between your thumb and index finger. Use your other hand to pierce the skin near the stem using your fingernail or a small knife. Once you've made a hole, peel back the skin to reveal the white flesh inside. Key tip: Be careful not to damage the delicate flesh while removing the skin. If possible, try to keep the thin membrane attached to the skin intact. **Step 4: Separating the Flesh** Gently pull the white flesh away from the seed. You can use your teeth to help separate it if needed. Discard the seed and eat only the white flesh. Guideline: Some people prefer to suck out the juice first before separating the flesh from the seed. Either way is acceptable. **Step 5: Enjoying the Flavor** Savor the sweet and creamy flavor of the rambutan flesh. It has a texture similar to grapes or lychees and pairs well with chilled drinks. Key tip: Rambutans are best consumed at room temperature as refrigeration can dull their flavors. **Step 6: Storing Unfinished Rambutans** If you don't finish all your rambutans in one sitting, store them in an airtight container in the fridge. They should last up to three days. Guideline: Always check for signs of spoilage such as mold or off odor before consuming stored rambutans. By following these steps, you can enjoy the delightful taste and texture of rambutan without any difficulty! And when did you get this from your Daddy? Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved ice cream, especially the creamy one. One day, she asked her mom to buy her some ice cream. She wanted to enjoy her food. But her mom made her eat quickly. After dinner, Lily felt sick. She had eaten too fast. Her mom scolded her and said, "Next time, don't take so long with your ice cream." Lily felt sad and regretful. She wished she had listened to her mom and hurried back home. Nauru was one of three great phosphate rock islands in the Pacific Ocean (the others were Banaba (Ocean Island) in Kiribati and Makatea in French Polynesia). The phosphate reserves on Nauru are now almost entirely depleted. Phosphate mining in the central plateau has left a barren terrain of jagged limestone pinnacles up to high. Mining has stripped and devastated about 80 per cent of Nauru's land area, and has also affected the surrounding Exclusive Economic Zone; 40 per cent of marine life is estimated to have been killed by silt and phosphate runoff. [6.4 sec, silence, then S crawls some more] Dress Up a Salad with Edible Flowers: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction Adding edible flowers to your salad can transform an ordinary dish into a visually stunning and flavorful experience. This guide will take you through the process of selecting, preparing, and incorporating edible flowers into your salads, providing helpful tips and guidelines along the way. By following these steps, you'll be able to create beautiful, delicious, and eye-catching dishes that are sure to impress. Step 1: Choosing Edible Flowers The first step is to select edible flowers suitable for consumption. It's crucial to choose flowers from reputable sources or grow them yourself to ensure they haven't been treated with chemicals or pesticides. Some popular edible flower options include nasturtiums, violets, calendulas, borage, marigolds, lavender, chamomile, hibiscus, roses, and sunflowers. Before using any flower, double-check its edibility by consulting reliable resources such as trusted gardening books, websites, or local agricultural extension offices. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Always confirm the plant's identity before consuming; some non-edible plants have toxic lookalikes. * Avoid picking flowers from florists, nurseries, or roadsides since they may contain harmful substances like chemical fertilizers, herbicides, or vehicle exhaust residue. * Choose organic flowers whenever possible. Why this step matters: Consuming non-edible flowers could lead to illness or allergies, so proper identification and sourcing are essential. Step 2: Harvesting and Preparation Harvest edible flowers early in the day after dew has evaporated but while temperatures remain cool. Gently snip off blossoms, leaving behind leaves and stems to promote future growth. Rinse flowers under cold water gently to remove dirt and insects, then dry thoroughly between layers of paper towels or a clean cloth. Remove sepals (the green leafy part at the base) if necessary, depending on their taste and texture. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Handle flowers delicately during harvesting and cleaning to prevent bruising and damage. * Discard wilted or discolored petals. * If growing your own flowers, avoid harvesting immediately after applying fertilizer or pest control products. Wait several days or until recommended intervals specified by product labels. Why this step matters: Proper handling ensures freshness and preserves both visual appeal and flavor. Cleaning removes potential contaminants, reducing food safety risks. Step 3: Storing Edible Flowers He has a lot of heavy suit on... Uh why do you think that's bad to waste time Ross? Becoming good at RoBeats, a popular rhythm game on the Roblox platform, requires practice, patience, and an understanding of its mechanics. Here's a comprehensive guide that will help you improve your skills: **Step 1: Familiarize Yourself with the Game Interface** Start by getting acquainted with the RoBeats interface. This includes the note highway (where notes come from), the judgment line (the point where you hit notes), the background visualizer, and the pause button. Understanding these elements is crucial as they are integral to playing the game effectively. **Step 2: Learn How to Time Your Hits Accurately** The core mechanic of RoBeats involves hitting incoming notes as they cross the judgment line. To do this accurately, listen carefully to the beat of the song and watch for cues on when to press the corresponding keys (usually marked as blue or white squares). Aim for 'Perfect' judgments which give maximum points. **Key Tip:** Start practicing with simple songs before moving onto more complex ones. This gradual progression allows you to get used to timing without overwhelming yourself. **Step 3: Master Key Pressing Techniques** In RoBeats, there are three types of notes - single tap, hold, and slide. Each type requires different techniques: - **Single Tap Notes**: These are individual squares that require one quick tap when they reach the judgment line. Practice quick reaction times and precise timing for these. - **Hold Notes**: These last longer than regular notes, requiring you to keep pressing until they disappear offscreen. Focus on maintaining consistent pressure during holds. - **Slide Notes**: Slides need you to move along a path following the directional arrow while holding down the assigned key(s). Train your hand-eye coordination for smooth slides. **Step 4: Utilize Both Hands Effectively** RoBeats uses up to six keys simultaneously. Make sure you distribute them evenly between both hands, using fingers for adjacent keys. For example, if you have two keys close together, consider assigning them to your index and middle finger rather than stretching out one finger over two keys. **Step 5: Increase Speed Gradually** As you become comfortable with basic timings and multi-key combos, increase the speed setting gradually. Rapid speeds can be challenging but provide greater rewards upon successful completion. Remember though, rushing into high speeds may lead to mistakes; take time to adjust. **Step 6: Engage in Regular Training Sessions** Consistency is vital in mastering any skill, including RoBeats. Set aside some time daily to play and train. Repetition reinforces muscle memory, helping you react instinctively instead of having to think about every action. **Step 7: Stay Updated With New Songs & Features** Developers frequently add new content, so stay updated! Playing fresh tracks keeps things interesting and tests your adaptability against varying rhythms and patterns. **Guideline:** Always remember to enjoy the process. Frustration often arises when we push ourselves too hard or focus solely on results. In the middle of the room, place a large rock that's too heavy for the children to lift. When the children arrive, ask if they notice anything new in the classroom. Talk about the rock and how it might've gotten Say: This rock is taking up too much room in our class. I think we should move it out of the way. Let each child try to move it. Say: Some rocks are just too heavy for us to lift. There are many rocks that are too heavy for grownups to lift. This rock is too heavy to move and it'll have to stay here until we can get some help. (If possible, take children outside to look for rocks or things too heavy for any adult to lift.) Leave the rock in the middle of the room for the rest of the lesson. I want you to find one rock and then come back to our big rock. After children each find a rock, have them examine their rock and tell something they notice about it. It may have sparkles, holes or an interesting color or shape. 4. Rock Scramble Tell children each to look at their rock one last time and then put it into a pile by the large rock. Mix up all the rocks. Say: Now I want you to find your rock. Familiarize yourself with the level layout: Before attempting to solve any puzzle, take some time to observe your surroundings carefully. On Level 1 of 100 Floors Season's Revenge (also known as 100 Rooms), you will find yourself inside an elevator. The door is locked, and there are four buttons - one red button at the bottom right corner labeled 'Help,' another three numbered buttons above it (1-2-3). Your goal here is to escape this room by unlocking the main door. 2. Understand the mechanics of the game: Each floor has its unique puzzle that needs solving before moving onto the next level. Most solutions require using objects found within the rooms or manipulating various aspects of the environment itself. Some interactions may need specific timing or sequence, so patience and observation can be crucial. 3. Examine the Red Help Button: Tap on the red help button located at the bottom right corner. A question mark symbol appears asking if you want hints. For now, ignore it because we are going to figure out this level without assistance. But remember, these hints become useful when you get stuck later in the game. 4. Experiment with Numbered Buttons: Now let's focus on those three numbered buttons (1-2-3) above the help button. There seems to be no visible reaction upon pressing them individually. However, try tapping all three numbers quickly in succession (1-2-3). This action triggers something interesting! You hear a sound like a lock being released along with the appearance of a green checkmark over the middle button (number 2). 5. Press the Green Checkmark Button: After successfully performing the combination (1-2-3), press the middle button again, which now displays a green checkmark. As a result, the primary exit door opens, signifying that you have solved Level 1! Key Tips & Guidelines: - Pay close attention to details such as colors, shapes, sounds, etc., since they often provide clues towards solution. - Don't rush through levels; instead, take your time observing every element in the room. - Make mental notes of patterns discovered during gameplay as they might prove beneficial in future stages. - Use the hint system sparingly as frequent usage could lead to unintended spoilers. - Remember combinations, sequences, or codes encountered throughout the game as they tend to reappear in subsequent levels. Creating a sum of data in Google Sheets is a fundamental skill that can help you efficiently manage and analyze your spreadsheet information. This tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step with clear explanations and helpful tips along the way. **Step 1: Open Google Sheets** First, open Google Sheets by visiting <https://sheets.google.com/> and signing into your Google account if necessary. If you don't have an existing document, click on 'Blank' to create a new one. **Step 2: Enter Your Data** Enter the numerical values or formulas you want to include in the sum into individual cells within the same column or row. Make sure there are no blank rows or columns interrupting your dataset as this might affect the accuracy of your total. For example, let's assume we have sales figures for January through April in cells B2 to B5: | | A | B | |---|-----------|---------| | 1 | Month | Sales | | 2 | January | 5000 | | 3 | February | 6000 | | 4 | March | 7000 | | 5 | April | 8000 | **Key Tip**: Always ensure your numbers are formatted correctly (as numbers) so they can be added up without issues. You can check their format by clicking on the cell, then selecting 'Format' > 'Number' from the menu bar. **Step 3: Select The Range** Select all the cells containing the numeric data that you wish to add together. To do this, simply click and drag across these cells using your mouse pointer. Using our previous example, select cells B2 through B5. **Step 4: Use The SUM Function** Type `=SUM(` followed by the selected range of cells enclosed within parentheses. For instance, based on our earlier example, type `=SUM(B2:B5)`. The colon (`:`) indicates that you're referring to a continuous range of cells. It's crucial to get the starting and ending points right because incorrect ranges may lead to errors or wrong results. **Step 5: Press Enter** After typing the formula, press Enter to calculate the sum of the selected range. Google Sheets will automatically display the result in the active cell where you entered the formula. In our case, after pressing Enter, cell B6 should now show the value `26000`, which represents the total sales figure for January through April. **Key Guideline**: Remember that any changes made to the original data will update the sum dynamically since formulas recalculate whenever a change occurs in related cells. By following these steps, you can quickly compute totals in Google Sheets, simplifying complex datasets and enhancing your analytical capabilities! Who takes you out for a walk? Title: Eliminate Cockroaches with Homemade Roach Balls ===================================================== A cockroach infestation can be a major problem in any household or business premises. These pests are not only disgusting but also carry diseases that can affect human health. While there are many commercial products available for roach control, they may contain harmful chemicals and might prove expensive in the long run. Fortunately, you can make your own roach balls using simple ingredients found around your home. This method is cost-effective, eco-friendly, and safe for pets and children when done correctly. Here's our comprehensive guide on eliminating cockroaches with homemade roach balls. ### Things You'll Need * 1 cup powdered sugar (attracts the roaches) * 1 cup baking soda (neutralizes stomach acids causing internal damage) * 1 cup plain flour (binds all ingredients together) * Water (to form dough) * Airtight container(s) for storage ### Step 1: Mix Dry Ingredients ------------------------------- Combine equal parts of powdered sugar, baking soda, and plain flour in a large bowl. Stir until well mixed. Powdered sugar acts as bait, attracting the roaches towards the mixture. Baking soda reacts with the acid present in the roaches' digestive system, producing gas which eventually kills them. Plain flour serves as a binding agent, holding everything else together. ### Step 2: Add Water Gradually ----------------------------- Slowly add water to the dry mix while stirring continuously. The aim is to create a pliable dough consistency similar to playdough. Be careful not to add too much water; if this happens, simply balance it out by adding more flour. ### Step 3: Form Dough Balls ------------------------- Once you have achieved the right consistency, start forming small balls about the size of marbles. Make sure these are uniform so that every roach gets an equal amount of both sugar and baking soda. Allow the balls to dry completely before proceeding. ### Step 4: Place Balls Strategically ---------------------------------- Identify areas where you usually see cockroaches such as kitchen cabinets, under sinks, near drains, cracks and crevices. Place the dried roach balls discreetly in these locations. Remember, the goal is to lure the roaches into consuming the deadly combination unknowingly. ### Key Tips & Guidelines * Always wash hands thoroughly after handling the roach balls. * Keep away from reach of children and pets. Although made from food-grade materials, ingestion could cause discomfort due to the high sodium content. * Regularly check and replace old or uneaten roach balls every two weeks. Over time, they lose their potency and become less effective. * Store unused dough or formed balls in airtight containers to prevent hardening or moisture absorption. * For severe infestations, consider combining this method with other strategies like sealing entry points, reducing clutter, and maintaining cleanliness. Remember, patience is key when dealing with pests like cockroaches. It might take several days or even weeks before you notice significant results. However, consistent application will yield positive outcomes over time. Good luck! Look at all these things to play with. You said that would pay me tonight. Okay I'll just read him a little bit. Now I want to extend that graph to the left and to the right, and I'm going to need some facts from before. First of all I have a table of values that I found before. Tangent of 0 is 0, tangent of pi over 4 is 1 and tangent of pi over 2 is undefined. And this is what gives us that vertical asymptote at x equals pi over 2. I also need 2 facts. The identity tangent of -x equals negative tangent x and that tangent has period pi. Now this this fact means that if you take at 2 inputs that have opposite values you get opposite outputs. So, for example if I plug negative pi over 4 into tangent, I get the opposite output -1. For tangent's an odd function. Of course the opposite of 0 is 0 gives me the opposite output which is also 0 and the opposite input negative pi over 2, it's just it's still going to be undefined. And this helps me complete a single period of tangent functions. And once you've got them drawn it's super easy to draw the tangent curve. Just remember the shape. And then you can put your asymptotes on. There are going to be asymptotes every pi units. This asymptote is at 3 pi over 2, this one's at -3 pi over 2. That's the great thing about periodic functions because they're periodic, once you have one cycle drawn you just duplicate that cycle as many times as you need to. Title: How to Date a Twin: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Dating a twin can be an exciting yet challenging experience. Twins often have a unique bond that sets them apart from other siblings, which may require adjustments when forming a romantic relationship with one of them. This guide will provide you with essential steps, helpful tips, and crucial guidelines to navigate this intriguing journey while maintaining respect and understanding for both twins. Step 1: Understanding the Twin Bond Before pursuing a relationship with a twin, take time to learn about their special connection. Twins typically share a deep emotional bond, enhanced communication, and sometimes even telepathy-like intuition. They might finish each other's sentences or sense when something is wrong with their sibling. Recognizing this closeness will help you appreciate their relationship and avoid feeling threatened by it. Key Tip: Always remember that your partner's bond with their twin does not diminish their love for you; instead, it adds another layer of complexity and richness to their life experiences. Step 2: Identifying Individual Interests To truly connect with a twin, make sure to explore and acknowledge their individual interests, talents, and aspirations separate from those of their sibling. Encourage independent activities and hobbies, as well as shared ones, to foster a balanced relationship between you and your date. Guideline: Be mindful of comparing your twin partner to their sibling based on commonalities or differences. Instead, celebrate what makes them unique and cherish the moments you spend together. Step 3: Communicating Openly About Your Intentions When expressing your feelings towards a twin, ensure honesty and transparency regarding your intentions. Being upfront about wanting a serious commitment or simply enjoying casual dating can prevent misunderstandings down the line. Key Tip: If possible, engage in conversations about relationships separately with each twin to understand their perspectives on love, partnership, and family dynamics. Step 4: Respecting Boundaries As you get closer to one twin, it is vital to establish clear boundaries within the triad (you, the twin, and their sibling). These limits should include privacy concerns, time spent together versus alone, and involvement in personal matters. Make sure all parties involved are comfortable with these arrangements to maintain harmony and trust. Guideline: Regularly check in with yourself and your partner(s) to assess whether existing boundaries need modification or reinforcement. Flexibility is essential in adapting to changing circumstances and emotions. Step 5: Navigating Family Gatherings Family events involving both twins can be overwhelming at first, especially if they possess strong telepathic connections. To ease any anxiety, familiarize yourself with each twin's role within the family unit, observe their interactions, and participate in group discussions without favoritism or bias. Key Tip: Avoid treating twins interchangeably during social gatherings - address them individually, recognize their distinct qualities, and show genuine interest in getting to know each person better. Conclusion: Building a successful relationship with a twin requires patience, empathy, and open communication. By following the steps outlined above and remaining attuned to your own needs as well as those of your partner(s), you can create a harmonious balance between nurturing your love story and honoring the unbreakable bond between twins. Ethics tries to answer questions like: A visual field test is an essential examination used by eye care professionals to detect blind spots (scotomas) in a patient's vision that could indicate various medical conditions such as glaucoma, stroke, brain tumors, or other neurological disorders. This step-by-step guide will provide you with all necessary information about performing a visual field test at home using affordable and accessible tools. **Things You'll Need:** * A computer or mobile device with internet access * A printed version of an Amsler grid (available online) * A well-lit room * Reading glasses (if needed) * Approximately 10 minutes of your time _Please note: While this self-administered test can help identify potential issues, it does not replace professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment from an eye care specialist._ ### Step 1: Familiarize Yourself With The Amsler Grid The Amsler grid consists of horizontal and vertical lines forming small squares, with a central fixation point in the middle. It resembles graph paper with a dot at its center. When looking directly at the dot, any distortion or interruption in the straightness of the lines indicates possible vision problems. ![Amsler Grid](https://www.amslergrid.org/wp-content/uploads/2016/04/AMSLERGRID-300x257.jpg) <small>Image source: <a href="https://www.amslergrid.org">AmslerGrid.org</a></small> ### Step 2: Adjust Your Lighting And Position Ensure the testing area has sufficient lighting but avoid direct sunlight or bright lamps shining into your eyes. Hang the printout of the Amsler grid approximately arm's length away and at eye level. If you wear reading glasses, put them on before proceeding. ### Step 3: Cover One Eye Close one eye gently with your hand while keeping the other open and focused on the central fixation point of the grid. Make sure no stray light enters around your fingers. ### Step 4: Observe The Lines Around The Central Fixation Point While staring at the central dot, take notice if any of the surrounding lines appear wavy, blurry, discolored, missing, or distorted. Pay particular attention to the corners and edges of the grid. ### Step 5: Document Any Abnormalities Found If you observe irregularities, mark them down immediately, noting their location and shape. Repeat the process for the other eye. ### Step 6: Compare Results To Previous Tests Regularly perform this test to monitor changes in your visual fields over time. Comparing results may reveal patterns suggestive of underlying health concerns requiring further investigation by an ophthalmologist or optometrist. ## Key Tips & Guidelines * Perform the test under consistent conditions to ensure accurate comparisons between sessions. * Regularly schedule comprehensive eye examinations with qualified healthcare providers even if you perceive normal vision during these tests. * Consult a doctor promptly if you notice sudden changes in your vision, persistent headaches, floaters, flashes of light, or difficulty distinguishing colors. * Remember that although useful, this method cannot fully replace professional diagnostic tools and expertise. By following this thorough tutorial, you now possess the knowledge required to conduct a basic visual field assessment using an Amsler grid. However, always prioritize regular checkups with certified eye care specialists who have access to advanced technology and clinical experience necessary for maintaining optimal visual health. Cut out all the CVC (consonant, vowel, consonant) words and hold them in a pile. Sit in front of your child and show them one of the words (for example, man). Then show them some of the other words; when you show them a word they've already seen (like man) again they shout 'Full Circle'! The Full Circle exercise will help encourage your child to read the CVC words at a glance. They are looking at the letters in the words and the shape of the words - this is what learning to read is largely about! Loud voice as if giving a speech Tim and Liz are friends.
